classes.name: "Cultivating Presence in the Garden": Storytelling Workshops with Roundabout Theatre Company
classes.desc: Come to Poppa & Momma Jones Historical Garden for storytelling workshops by the Roundabout Theatre Company! Join the garden group on Tuesday, July 21st for Physical Performance in Bloom workshop and Tuesday, July 28th for Soundscaping in the Garden workshop.

classes.name: 07/24 Wingate In-Person Care Captain Training
classes.desc: This in-person training and street tree care activity will teach you to become a 'Care Captain' and empower you to help protect and care for street trees. Learn arboricultural principles and how they can be applied to caring for NYC's urban forest, while activating your community for environmental health and resilience. Upon completing the training, attendees will be eligible for their free Care Captain toolkit! This event has been funded in part by Council Member Rita Joseph, District 40.

classes.name: 11BC Serenity Garden Presents Mary Edwards
classes.desc: Mary Edwards will be performing her soundscape compositions which combine electroacoustic modeling, conceptualized space data sonification, interactive wordless intonations and her field recordings at our garden.

classes.name: 40 in Focus: SummerStage through the Lens of Photographer Jack Vartoogian
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM
classes.desc:
From 1987 to 2019, celebrated NYC photographer Jack Vartoogian captured hundreds of SummerStage performances across Manhattan parks, from Central Park to Marcus Garvey Park and Tompkins Square Park. This selection of intimate, rarely seen imagery gives viewers a look back at past decades of outdoor live music in New York City, a peek behind the curtain with never-before-seen backstage photos and honors those artists who have passed on. From the earliest performances with Sun Ra Arkestra and Mavis and Pop Staples, to legendary artists in memoriam like Tito Puente and Hugh Masekela and backstage shots of Buddy Guy and Darlene Love, this exhibition celebrates not only a photographer's commitment to documenting performance but also celebrates a 40-year legacy of SummerStage.

This free exhibition is presented by City Parks Foundation in collaboration with NYC Parks Art & Antiquities department.

An opening reception will be held on June 16 from 6pm-8 pm, offering light refreshments, music and an exclusive exhibition poster while supplies last.

Image credit: Sun Ra Arkestra at SummerStage, 1989, Jack Vartoogian

classes.name: 7/24 Restoration Fridays at Forest Park
classes.desc:

Registration is required. All tools will be provided, and no prior experience is needed. Just bring your enthusiasm. Please come dressed in sturdy, closed-toe shoes and bring your own water bottle.

classes.name: All That Skitters!

classes.desc: How does the Fiddler Crab survive in the salt marsh tides? What other crabs live in the marsh? What happens in the winter? Where do Horseshoe Crabs fit in? Explore the complexities and threats to the fragile salt marsh with Mike Feller, retired Chief Naturalist of the NYC Parks Department. Sponsored by the Salt Marsh Alliance in partnership with the Marine Park Alliance

We will also explore the flora and fauna of Willowbrook Park. All equipment will be provided. Ages 8 and older. Lottery registration opens Monday July 27.

classes.location: Kids Playground - Large (in Washington Square Park)
classes.desc: Draw! Cut! Paste! Smile! Kids of all ages are invited to join our resident playground best friend Miss Debbie for art-making in the playground.

ages 50 years-old and older to enroll in the, BE BOLDER: Ballet Explorations (BE) Ballet Offers Life-long Discovery, Expression, and Reconnection (BOLDER), dance program, a fun ballet class with live music. The program reflects Misty Copeland's deeply held belief that dance is for every body–and every stage of life. This program is FREE.

Please email sarah.bishow@parks.nyc.gov to register.

classes.name: Astoria Pool: Lap Swim
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 7:00 AM - 8:30 AM
classes.desc: Take part in a long-distance lap swim competition. To participate in the competition, please speak to an Aquatics Specialist on the pool deck and indicate this on your application. An awards ceremony will be held at the end of August to celebrate the health and fitness achievements of swimmers who complete 25 miles or more. Visit https://www.nycgovparks.org/events/lap-swim to register.

classes.name: August Project WASTE
classes.desc: Join us for our upcoming Project WASTE event! This event is part of our hands-on outreach strategy which includes trash collection and assessment. As a volunteer, you will help our Ecology staff count, assess and remove trash from the Muskrat Cove trash boom so that we can categorize floatable trash into distinct categories while keeping the river clean. Information on the quantity, toxicity, material, and brands of trash will help to identify sources of pollution and to inform communities, businesses and municipal officials of this Bronx River problem. The event will take place in-water at the Muskrat Cove trash boom. The activity will start around 10:00am and last until 12:00pm. All materials will be provided. Nearby transportation includes the 2 train at 233rd Street and the Metro-North at Woodlawn. The meeting location will be the entrance to the jogging path just to the left of the station parking lot. Event Contact Info: Christian Murphy (917) 361-8922 Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

\r

classes.name: Best Special Kids: Horticulture and Art Workshop with Gisselle Ramirez
classes.desc: Join Giselle of Best Special Kids for this 1.5-2hr workshop that engages children with disabilities and their families in a sensory-friendly horticulture and art experience. Participants will decorate nursery pots and plant herbs or small vegetables, combining creativity with hands-on learning. The workshop is designed to be inclusive and accessible, with adapted materials, visual supports, and flexible participation. Caregivers are encouraged to participate, strengthening family engagement and reinforcing learning at home. Through this workshop, participants will build fine motor and creative skills, learn basic gardening practices, and develop confidence in a supportive environment. The session also introduces families to ongoing community-based, nature-centered programming. Our goal is to create an inclusive space where children can connect with nature, express themselves, and build meaningful skills while fostering a sense of belonging. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! classes.name: Bioblitz: Concrete Plant Park to Starlight Park with RJ Hawkins
classes.desc: Join RJ for a BioBlitz event that will teach Bronx residents how to document wildlife and biodiversity along the Bronx River using the iNaturalist app. Participants will take part in a guided exploration between local parks while learning about urban ecology and how citizen science can support long-term conservation and restoration efforts in NYC. Building on last year’s success, this year’s event will include expanded outreach and prizes to the top 3 individuals who log the most observations on iNaturalist to encourage greater participation and engagement. The goals of the workshop are to increase community involvement in biodiversity monitoring, strengthen ecological literacy, and empower participants to become long-term environmental stewards of the Bronx River ecosystem. Expected outcomes include documenting hundreds of wildlife observations, increasing participation in citizen science, and helping community members gain a deeper understanding of local wildlife, habitat restoration, and the role residents can play in protecting urban ecosystems. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Bronx River City of Water Day Celebration
classes.desc:
Join the Bronx River Alliance on Saturday July 25, from 12-4pm at Starlight Park, to celebrate City of Water Day! We're coming together to celebrate our waterfronts and help build resilient, connected communities.

There are so many ways to enjoy this free family-friendly event! Activities include:Learn about the wildlife of the Bronx River on a Guided Nature WalkHydration Station with teas foraged from the Bronx River FoodwayInteractive Community DrummingTea and Healing Sound BathFresh Produce DistributionIndigenous Art Workshop with the North American Indigenous Center of New YorkCanoe-Painting Art ProjectWeaving WorkshopCanoeing River Cleanup (registration required)And more!

classes.name: Brooklyn Bridge Park Conservancy Waterfront Walks
classes.desc:
Join a free walking tour to learn about the history of the Brooklyn waterfront at Brooklyn Bridge Park! Learn about the local ecology, Brooklyn Bridge Park's sustainable design, and how the park came to life! Tours last roughly 60-90 minutes, starting from the entrance of Pier 1 and finishing near Pier 3.

Advance registration is required. Registration opens a month in advance of each date. Please note this event is subject to change due to weather or other extenuating circumstances.

classes.name: Brooklyn Bridge Park Movies With A View presented by Persol
classes.location: Brooklyn Bridge Park Pier 1 Brooklyn, NY 11201 (in Brooklyn Bridge Park)
classes.desc:
Brooklyn Bridge Park's free outdoor film series, Movies With A View presented by Persol, is back for 2026! Enjoy free screenings on Thursday evenings in July and August – expanding with an additional family film night on Friday, September 18.

This summer's lineup features beloved classics and modern hits, taking place across the globe from Italy to Costa Rica and back to New York City. With a diverse blend of genres, including action, romantic comedy, and a musical, there is something for everyone to enjoy.

Each evening, the lawn opens at 6:00 PM and the movie begins at sunset. Entry is free on a first-come, first-served basis.

Thursday, July 2: Hamilton (PG-13, 2020, 160 minutes) – Kick off the 4th of July weekend with New York's own Lin-Manuel Miranda's imaginative retelling of American founding father Alexander Hamilton. This is the filmed version of the Broadway production – singing along is encouraged!

Thursday, July 9: When Harry Met Sally (R, 1989, 95 minutes) – This classic romantic comedy starring Meg Ryan and Billy Crystal attempts to answer the question, "Can men and women just be friends?". Directed by the late Rob Reiner.

Thursday, July 16:  Bend it like Beckham (PG-13, 2002, 112 minutes) – Leading up to the World Cup Final, revisit the popular film about a young woman chasing her passion for soccer despite her family's wishes.

Thursday, July 23: The Talented Mr. Ripley (R, 1999, 139 minutes) – Based on the bestselling novel, this psychological thriller sets Matt Damon, Jude Law, and Gwyneth Paltrow against the Italian coast, making it the perfect film to watch on a summer evening in the park.

Thursday, July 30: Clueless (PG-13, 1995, 97 minutes) – The beloved film adapted from Jane Austen's Emma about a high school student playing matchmaker for her peers and teachers is a perfect movie to watch on the lawn with friends.

Thursday, August 6: Jurassic Park (PG-13, 1993, 122 minutes) – The first film of the epic franchise thirty years in the making, revisit the original movie when scientists first set foot on Isla Nubar and encounter the adventure of a lifetime.

Thursday, August 13: Do the Right Thing (R, 1989, 120 minutes) – Directed by Brooklyn's Spike Lee, this classic film follows the stories of various Bed-Stuy residents on a hot summer day, culminating in violence and tragedy.

Thursday, August 20: Bridesmaids (R, 2011, 125 minutes) – This box-office hit comedy features career-defining performances by Kristen Wiig, Maya Rudolph, Rose Byrne, Melissa McCarthy and more.

Thursday, August 27: Public Choice – In early August, the public gets to vote for their favorite selection from three options to view on August 27! Selections include Challengers (R, 2024, 131 minutes), The Great Gatsby (PG-13, 2013, 143 minutes), and Ocean's Eleven (PG-13, 2001, 116 minutes). Voting takes place online at brooklynbridgepark.org.

Friday, September 18: How to Train Your Dragon (PG, 2010, 93 minutes) – Extending to our first September viewing, join for family film night with this fun animated story about a young Viking who befriends a dragon.

This year's line-up of free screenings is presented by Persol. Throughout the series, Persol will bring the spirit of the Italian Riviera to Brooklyn Bridge Park through immersive on-site activations inspired by the effortless elegance, and cinematic atmosphere of the brand's Spring/Summer 2026 campaign and collection.

Guests are encouraged to arrive early to grab a spot at Pier 1 Harbor View Lawn in Brooklyn Bridge. Guests are welcome to bring their own food for picnicking and encouraged to support nearby Park concessions and neighborhood restaurants. Popcorn, water, soft drinks, and alcoholic beverages (for those 21+) will be available for sale on-site by Fornino. Attendees are encouraged to bring picnic blankets or small soft cushions but reminded that lawn chairs and dogs are not permitted on the lawn, and glass bottles and outside alcohol are never allowed in the Park. For full details, please visit brooklynbridgepark.org/event-series/movies-with-a-view

classes.name: Bryant Park Author Series: Speculative Fiction
classes.desc:
Catch a glimpse of imaginary worlds at our Speculative Fiction author panel with Clarence A. Haynes, The Broken Hearts Agency; Isabel J. Kim, Sublimation; Caitlin Rozakis, Startup Hell; and Leila Siddiqui, The Glowing Hours.

Clarence A. Haynes, a native New Yorker, is author of the urban fantasy glam horror novel The Ghosts of Gwendolyn Montgomery and coauthor of actor/producer Omar Epps's lauded sci-fi/fantasy series Nubia: The Awakening and The Reckoning. He is also the author of the middle-grade nonfiction work The Legacy of Jim Crow. In addition to working as a freelance editor, Haynes has penned articles for Newsday, LitHub, The Root, and TheGrio, among other publications. He resides in Brooklyn, New York.

Isabel J. Kim lives near New York City in an apartment filled with books and swords. She is the author of numerous short stories and has won the Nebula, Locus, BSFA and the Shirley Jackson Awards. Her work has been translated into multiple languages and reprinted in multiple best of the year anthologies. When she's not writing, she's co-hosting Wow If True, a podcast about internet culture.

Caitlin Rozakis is a New York Times best-selling author who writes fantasy with a satirical twist and a cozy heart. Her debut novel Dreadful turned out not to be dreadful at all. Her Everyday Enchantments series include standalones The Grimoire Grammar School Parent Teacher Association and Startup Hell. She lives in Jersey City with her husband and son.

Leila Siddiqui is a Chicago-born Texan who calls New York home. She is currently the co-host of Come Play with Us, a spooky storytelling podcast. At this year's BookCon, Leila moderated an onstage interview with New York Times bestselling author R. F. Kuang, and she will soon be a featured panelist and author at Pittsburgh Book Festival and Dark Summer Book Fest. She lives with her partner in Queens.

Hosted by Lisa Burdige, writer and influencer.

Produced in partnership with Rob Cameron, author, educator, and lead organizer for Brooklyn Speculative Fiction Writers

classes.name: Bryant Park Picnic Performance: Carnegie Hall Citywide: El Laberinto del Coco
classes.desc:
Enjoy a free, Friday-night concert by the Afro–Puerto Rican bomba fusion innovators El Laberinto del Coco, led by master percussionist Hector "Coco" Barez. Blending jazz, funk, reggae, and numerous Latin influences with traditional bomba elements and instruments such as the barril and cuá, the band expands the boundaries of bomba while staying true to its intoxicating essence. Experience a high-energy sonic tapestry that has attracted fans around the world.

El Laberinto del Coco is an Afro-Puerto Rican Bomba Fusion ensemble founded by visionary percussionist Hector "Coco" Barez in 2017. With a unique blend of sizzling musical ideas, Barez presents new ways to the traditional sounds of Puerto Rican Bomba. After touring the world with Ballet Folklorico de Puerto Rico "Areyto," Grammy Award-winning groups like Calle 13, Bacilos and world-renowned bands like Bio Ritmo, Miramar, William Cepeda and Bayanga, Barez decides to embark on a new journey.

In 2017, Hector Barez received a commission from the Puerto Rican Institute of Culture and the National Endowment for the Arts to record El Laberinto del Coco's first album. With critics comparing this recording to Rafael Cortijo's masterpiece The Time Machine, a new era in Puerto Rican music is established. At performances, Barez brings to life a unique concept with a very distinctive sound with rich syncopated horn lines, funky guitar licks, solid multilayered percussion and enchanting voices, making El Laberinto del Coco a fan favorite. During the COVID-19 pandemic, El Laberinto del Coco kept busy releasing singles and keeping people's hopes alive through virtual concerts.

Carnegie Hall is honored to partner with local community organizations to present free concerts that are sensational celebrations of sounds from across the country and around the globe. At a Carnegie Hall Citywide concert, you can revisit your favorite music or try something new, stay in your home borough or journey farther afield. Whatever you choose, you'll experience everything that makes New York City so vibrant.

Bryant Park Picnic Performances presented by Bank of America is a free outdoor festival that welcomes all New Yorkers to experience the city's vibrant arts and culture. The series provides a platform for extraordinary artists and serves as a vital outdoor venue for a wide array of New York's cultural institutions.

classes.name: Bryant Park Picnic Performance: Carnegie Hall Citywide: NYC Ska Orchestra
classes.desc:
Experience the joy of a live performance by the NYC Ska Orchestra, which blends big-band jazz, soul, and R&B with the iconic Jamaican music styles of ska, mento, rock steady, reggae, and more. Led by celebrated trumpeter, singer, arranger, and educator Kevin Batchelor, this 20-piece big band also features legendary Jamaican percussionist-vocalist Larry MacDonald and Jamaican master drummer Carl Wright, who together have worked with Peter Tosh, Bob Marley, Bunny Wailer, Lee "Scratch" Perry, the Skatalites, Toots and the Maytals, and many others.

NYC Ska Orchestra is a 20-piece big band that creates and performs lush original compositions and arrangements of songs from the rich, historic, and culturally-iconic Jamaican music styles of ska, mento, rock steady, reggae, and jazz of the 1950s, 60s and 70s. They blend the exuberant musical layers of the jazz big band era, soul and R&B, with Jamaican roots music; effortlessly flowing into a unique, accessible, joyous and danceable sound that audiences love.

NYC Ska Orchestra is led by arranger, trumpeter, singer and educator Kevin Batchelor; and features 87 year-young celebrated Jamaican percussionist/vocalist Larry MacDonald, and Jamaican master drummer Carl Wright. Together they have recorded and/or performed with nearly every great Jamaican music artist including Peter Tosh, Bob Marley, Bunny Wailer, Lee Scratch Perry, Skatalites, and Toots & The Maytals; and they regularly work with the leading Jamaican artists today.

The orchestra also features singers Pallavi Gummalam and singer/arranger Aida Brandies Hargrove, plus all top-notch pro players from NYC; which combines for a hugely entertaining, danceable, unforgettable show.

Classes are every Tuesday morning, 7:30am-8:30am, from April 25 to August 28, and are led by teachers and students who train nearby at CK Chu Tai Chi under the direction of Master Hyland Harris. Classes meet on the Upper Terrace, and are rain or shine. All levels are welcome, and no equipment is necessary. *Class on 6/23 and 6/30 will be moved to the Fountain Terrace.

Join NYC Park's Piping Plover Team and other community partners for a day of educational activities and crafts to help us better understand how we can all share the beach and support a healthy living environment. Event is for all ages, with activities geared to younger audiences.

classes.name: Cinema Under the Stars: The Price of the Ticket (1989) by Karen Thorsen
classes.location: James Baldwin Lawn (in St. Nicholas Park),135th Street and St. Nicholas Avenue (in St. Nicholas Park)
classes.desc:
AFF's 2026 Summer Series presents The Price of the Ticket with an introduction by the Director Karen Thorsen. The film is preceded by James Baldwin: From Another Place. Live jazz + film under the stars!

Join us for a special evening of film, music, and community in St. Nicholas Park as part of AFF's 2026 Summer Series. Festivities begin at 6:30 PM with live jazz by David Lee Jones & The Harlem Groove Band. At 8:15 PM, we'll screen James Baldwin: From Another Place then The Price of the Ticket, a revelatory documentary showcasing archival footage and associates of James Baldwin. This emotional film expounds on Baldwin's legacy with appearances from Maya Angelou, Amiri Baraka, William Stryton, and others. Bring your blankets, lawn chairs, and loved ones for a night under the stars. The program is co-presented by Maysles Cinema, Harlem One Stop, and NYC Parks.

Free and open to all. In case of rain, the screening will take place at Maysles Cinema.

classes.name: Circus Arts in Herald Square Park
classes.desc: Try silk fan dancing, hoop tricks, contact juggling, plate spinning, and more! Our instructors can give you one-on-one guidance in a variety of flow and circus arts skills, or just drop by, pick up a prop, and have fun! All skill levels are welcome.

classes.name: City of Water Day at Kaiser Park
classes.desc: Join us for our annual City of Water Day event at Kaiser Park, where we will demonstrate expressions of resiliency and "Living With Water."

classes.name: City of Water Day at Kaiser Park
classes.desc: Join the Stewardship Team in celebrating City of Water Day at Kaiser Park! This is a large community event where many organizations will be tabling and other activities will be available. We will be leading a coastal cleanup as well as a seining demo. Come dressed in sturdy boots or shoes, long pants, and clothing that can get dirty. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 5 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.

classes.name: City of Water Day at Sunset Cove Park
classes.desc: This City of Water Day, volunteer with the Stewardship team to protect coastal areas of Sunset Cove Park! Attendees will help us remove debris that has washed ashore, contributing to the ongoing resilience of Sunset Cove's coastline. Please come dressed in sturdy boots or closed-shoes, long pants and clothing that can get dirty. Please DO NOT wear Crocs, sandals or shorts. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 10 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.

classes.name: City of Water Day with Friends of Inwood Hill Park
classes.location: Inwood Hill Nature Center (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc: Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day is a region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York–New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP), to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor. Friends of Inwood Hill Park is joining the Waterfront Alliance for another year to bring waterside programming to Inwood Hill Park alongside some of our favorite partner organizations, including the Urban Park Rangers, BioBus, the American Museum of Natural History, Riverkeeper, the New York Public Library, Creative Art Works, and more! We will meet in front of the Inwood Hill Nature Center for a full day of activities, including nature walks, trash cleanups, and more, to celebrate the water that connects our neighborhood and Inwood Hill Park to the larger Hudson River community and beyond.

classes.name: City of Water Day: Boardwalk Tidal Tales
classes.desc: Celebrate City of Water Day with our experienced Urban Park Rangers. Let's talk about intersections between land and shore at the most culturally active settlement at the mouth of the New York Harbor!

classes.name: City of Water Day: Island Hopping Canoeing Excursion
classes.location: Canoe and Kayak Launch (in Pelham Bay Park)
classes.desc: Its City of Water Day! Spend an afternoon exploring the islands that surround the Orchard Beach estuary by canoe. Experienced paddlers only. Participants are chosen by lottery. Lottery registration opens on Monday July 13.

classes.name: City of Water Day: Salt Marsh Exploration
classes.location: West 218th Street and Indian Road (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc: Join the Rangers for a special City of Water Day celebration with a guided walk along the Muscota Marsh. Discover the area's biodiversity as the Rangers highlight the birds, mammals, and other wildlife that call this unique habitat home

classes.name: City of Water Day: Saltwater Fishing
classes.location: Entrance-West Street and Greenpoint Avenue (in WNYC Transmitter Park)
classes.desc: Want to go fishing but don't know how or where to start? Join our Urban Park Rangers for free lessons! Learn how to properly use a fishing rod. Our experienced Rangers teach the ethics of fishing and the ecology of our waterways. All equipment will be provided. Recommended for ages 8 and older.

classes.name: City of Water Day: Silver Lake
classes.location: Forest Avenue and Silver Lake Park Road (in Silver Lake Park)
classes.desc: Celebrate City of Water Day with the Urban Park Rangers. Learn how Silver Lake supports local wildlife and connects to New York City's larger water system.

classes.name: City of Water Day: Soundview Park Guided Walk
classes.desc: Join NAC staff for an exploration of Soundview Park's Trails. We'll discuss plant identification and maritime woodlands. The Natural Areas Conservancy will host a guided walk on the trails at Soundview Park as part of City of Water Day in order to explore the trail and learn about our local ecosystem. Attendees will learn about plant id, maritime woodlands, and aquatic plants If you are attending, please fill out this volunteer waiver. All ages are welcome. Attendees under 18 will need to be accompanied by an adult. What you should bring: A water bottle Weather-appropriate clothing Sturdy, closed-toe shoes Insect repellent Long pants and tall socks recommended If you have any questions about this event or would like to learn more about the Natural Areas Conservancy, please visit naturalareasnyc.org, @naturalareasnyc on Instagram or contact Community Engagement Coordinator, Melissa Rosenthal at melissa.rosenthal@naturalareasnyc.org. Share on Twitter Share on Facebook Share on LinkedIn Share on Email Copy page URLCopied URL July 25, 2026 10:00 am - 1:00 pm Soundview Park Corner of Seward Ave & Metcalf Ave Natural Area Conservancy Sign Up(this link opens in new window)Get Directions(this link opens in new window) Events Through tours, lectures, and other happenings, the NAC brings New Yorkers together and helps them experience the city's 20,000 acres of forests, wetlands, and grasslands. 25 Jul City of Water Day: Soundview Park Guided Walk Soundview Park Saturday, 10:00am - 1:00pm 15 Aug Seton Falls Park Super Steward: Pathkeeper Training Seton Falls Park Saturday, 10:00am - 1:00pm Join us in our work to improve natural areas. As a community, we can push for proper funding, care, and management of these spaces, so they continue to provide immeasurable benefits to New York City and beyond. Subscribe to our newsletter to get the latest news, research, and events.

classes.name: Citywide Nursery Gardening
classes.desc: Join the Stewardship team and staff at Citywide Nursery to maintain our plant hoop houses and pot up perennials into larger grow pots. No experience necessary, staff will walk you through every part of the project. Come dressed in sturdy boots or shoes, long pants, and clothing that can get dirty. Please make sure to wear proper footwear as the nursery is very muddy and dusty. Space is limited and registration is required. This work is best suited for volunteers ages 13 and up. To sign up a group of 5 or more volunteers please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.

An awards ceremony will be held at the end of August to celebrate the health and fitness achievements of swimmers who complete 25 miles or more. Visit https://www.nycgovparks.org/events/lap-swim to register.

classes.name: Cocktails on the Farm
classes.desc:
Enjoy a relaxed evening at Randall's Island Park with Cocktails on our Urban Farm!

Sip on farm-fresh cocktails (21+) or complimentary mocktails, while enjoying plant-based bites, crafts, and activities. Plus, bring your clothing and textiles that need repair for a hands-on mending workshop with Repair Cafe El Barrio!

Afterwards, join us for Movie Night! Bring a blanket and settle in under the stars for an outdoor movie night in the Park. Enjoy sweeping views of the Hell Gate Bridge and the city skyline while watching Zootopia 2, the perfect pick for a fun summer evening!

Location: Urban Farm

For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

classes.name: Community Paddle at Starlight Park
classes.desc:
Join the Bronx River Alliance for a free community paddle at Starlight Park! Come see your neighborhood from the river's perspective!

This event is first come, first served with no pre-registration available.

"Waves" of paddlers will enjoy the river for about 30-40 minutes, after signing waivers and receiving a brief paddling safety orientation. A total of 4 waves of participants will be able to go on-water every hour of the event (11am, 12pm, 1pm, 2pm), with a maximum of about 15 participants per wave.

Staff and volunteers will be on-water to ensure safety, but please keep in mind that this is not a guided tour of the river.

Minors must be accompanied by an adult. Waivers for minors will need to be signed by an adult guardian.

classes.name: Community Work Day
classes.location: Wyckoff Farmhouse Museum (in Fidler-Wyckoff House Park)
classes.desc:
Roll up your sleeves and get your hands dirty at an upcoming volunteer day!

Stop by the Wyckoff Urban Farm on the fourth Saturday of every month from April-October between 11AM-1PM to to volunteer alongside our farm staff to care for our space and participate in special work projects. Learn about what we're growing, get to know our farmers, and get your hands dirty with farm projects!

Tickets are not required to attend, but it's always helpful to RSVP so we can plan projects accordingly.

THINGS TO KNOW:This event is family-friendly (ages 10+). We welcome individuals and families with kids ages 10 and over but children must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of their stay.Dress for the mess! Make sure to wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy in and wear close toed shoes to protect your feet.Come Prepared: We will provide all the tools and protective gear for your projects but coming prepared with a hat, long sleeved shirt, sunscreen, and bug spray is always a good idea.Facilities: Water fountain and public restrooms are available on-site. We recommend bringing snacks and a refillable water bottle.Weather: Community Work Days are rain or shine. If extreme weather is expected, we will update this page and contact those on the RSVP list to cancel.Volunteer Groups: This event is intended for individuals, families, and small groups of 5 or fewer participants. Community organizations and corporate volunteer groups are welcome to make advance reservations by emailing info@wyckoffmuseum.org to discuss scheduling options and availability.

classes.name: Cross Country Course Volunteer Days
classes.location: VCPA Garden & Compost Site (in Van Cortlandt Park)
classes.desc: Be a part of the community effort to maintain the historic Course Country Trail in Van Cortlandt ParkWhether you’re an experienced trailblazer or new to trail maintenance, your help will make a significant impact! Event Dates and Times: First Saturdays of the Month from May to August from 10am to 1pm.* *Please note that for groups of 10 or more only. Please email garcialeslie@vancortlandt.org to schedule a group. *Please note that due to the July 4th Holiday, the July event has been moved to the second Saturday. Meeting Details: Location: Van Cortlandt Park, VCPA Learning Garden, across from Riverdale Stables. Time: 10 am sharp; work begins at 10:15 am Upcoming Activities Include: Clearing debris and overgrowth Repairing trail structures Ensuring trail safety and accessibility What to Bring: Comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing Closed-toed shoes Water and a snack Why Join Us? Contribute to the vitality and safety of the trails you love! Engage with fellow community members, learn about trail maintenance, and enjoy the satisfaction of hands-on work in the great outdoors. Necessary tools and gloves will be provided! Want to bring a group? This event is limited to small groups of 9 or less. If you would like to bring more 10 or more people please reach out to garcialeslie@vancortlandt.org to schedule a separate event. Volunteer Policy for MinorsAges 15 – 17: A parent or guardian must sign and submit a volunteer waiver. Once the waiver is on file, the minor may participate without an accompanying adult.Ages 12 – 14: A parent or guardian must accompany the minor and remain on-site for the entire project.Under 12: Children may volunteer only at events specifically labeled family-friendly and must be accompanied by a parent or guardian at all times.Read moreGood to knowHighlightsIn personHighlightsIn personLocationVCPA Learning GardenBroadway & Mosholu Ave.

classes.name: Cyanotype Workshop with Nadia
classes.desc: Join Nadia and (re)discover how to make beautiful cyanotypes rooted in memory of places. After a quick introduction to cyanotype, we’ll reflect on our relationship to plants and the space. Then, we’ll select plants and items that correspond to our feelings to create our cyanotypes. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. *Note this event was originally scheduled for 7/11, but was rescheduled due to cloudy weather. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Fit & Lit with Janeil Mason
classes.location: On the High Line, under the Standard Hotel (in The High Line)
classes.desc:
Fit & Lit is your ultimate dance cardio class, where high-energy movement meets heart-pumping music for a workout experience like no other! Groove to the beats of Soca, Hip Hop, and Afrobeats–genres that'll keep your feet moving and your spirit lifted. We'll kick things off with an exciting cardio warm-up that'll have you dancing, sweating, and smiling all at once. Then, get ready for a quick, powerful HIIT section designed to boost your endurance and metabolism. But don't worry, we finish strong with a calming mindfulness practice to leave you feeling centered and refreshed.

No matter your fitness level, Fit and Lit celebrates rhythm, strength, and self-love–empowering you to dance your way to your best self. Join us, feel the music, feel ENERGIZED and get Fit and Lit!

Please wear comfortable clothes and shoes to move around in.

Free and open to the public. Advance registration is encouraged. Walk-ups are welcome. The event is rain or shine–the event space is fully covered. In the event of extreme weather, this program may be canceled for the health and safety of our visitors and staff. If a cancellation is necessary, Eventbrite registrants will be notified via email.

Tap into what the season has to offer. Foragers in the Foodway is a biweekly series, supported by New York State Agriculture and Markets. Attend one or all the events happening between June and September. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: From Plant to Paper with Sana Ashraf
classes.desc: Join us on the Bronx River with Sana Ashraf for plant-based paper making! While paper products today are largely mass produced, the techniques and guiding methodology are still rooted in its handmade origins. As such, this is a craft that can be easily adapted to a variety of materials, without investing in expensive equipment, and with a little creativity allows us to utilize and upcycle materials available in the plant world, our recycling bins, and even kitchen scraps intended for the compost pile. The session will involve a guided gathering of plant materials* from Concrete Plant Park, which will then be used to implement the process of plant based paper making. *The bulk of this will be focused on plants whose removal benefits the ongoing weeding and other stewardship works. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

Come visit the Backyard Garden after hours and join Full Moon Yoga Club for a special chair yoga session in the garden led by Barbara Kaminsky on the Thunder Moon!

Class start at 6:30 p.m. Gates open 30 min. before class.

RSVP in advance at: fullmoonyogaclub@protonmail.com Space is limited.

Please bring your own mat and water bottle and note there is no restroom on site.

About Barbara Kaminsky: Barbara has been teaching chair yoga since 2007. She is trained in Bikram, Anusara, and Chair Yoga and has also studied Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR). Her teaching is influenced by all of that as well as many workshops in Yoga Therapeutics and by the joy of moving with an open mind and heart. Chair yoga is for you if you prefer not to get up and down from the floor but still want the calming , stretching, breathing and relaxation that practice can bring. Each class will include a lively standing beginning (can also be done in a chair) seated yoga poses, breath work and meditation. As my first yoga teacher said, never too tired, never too old, never too stiff to begin again.

classes.name: Hart Island Tour (South Island)
classes.desc:
Hart Island is the largest public cemetery in the country and has served New York City since 1869. Hart Island's history is connected to many communities with important stories to tell. During the South Island Tour, Rangers will explore what Hart Island meant to the people who lived there, worked there, and were/are buried there. Walking the South Island includes discussion of an amusement park, military history, multiple pandemics, historic buildings and more. Join the Urban Park Rangers to hear their history and their voices.

Participants are chosen by lottery.

Lottery registration opens on Wednesday, July 15 and closes Thursday, July 16 at 11:59 p.m.

Please Note:
-You must register each member of your party for the lottery under the same account. Anyone who was not registered will not be permitted to attend.

- If you are selected for the program, you will receive a series of emails confirming your selection and must respond as soon as possible to avoid forfeiting your spot.

- Hart Island is only accessible via ferry. Selected participants will be provided details regarding program arrival and other specifics.

- There are no indoor facilities, restrooms or drinking fountains on Hart Island. There are portable restrooms on the island.

- This tour covers historical context of Hart Island and is about two hours of walking, some parts on uneven terrain. Please dress appropriately for the weather and the terrain.

- Program may be cancelled due to inclement weather at the discretion of ferry operations.

classes.desc: Riverside Playground Partners presents their annual summer concert series, fully funded by proceeds from the Hippo Playground Spring Fair.July 22 - Michael NapolitanoJuly 29 - Esther CrowAug 5 - Suzi SheltonAug 12 - Flor BromleyAug 19 - Matt Heaton Aug 26 - Hopalong Andrew Esther Crow is a NYC award-winning singer, songwriter, kids' entertainer, puppeteer and member of the Recording Academy. classes.name: Hudson Classical Theater Company presents: The Dancing Men
classes.location: Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument (in Riverside Park)
classes.when: Sun & Thu & Fri & Sat · 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM
classes.desc:
The Hudson Classical Theater Company Presents The Dancing Men: A Sherlock Holmes Mystery written by Susane Lee, adapted from the short story by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, and directed by Susane Lee and Nicholas Martin-Smith.

Hilton Cubitt seeks the help of Sherlock Holmes when he comes across a series of mysterious stick figure drawings resembling "dancing men" about his garden. These drawings are linked to his wife, Elsie Patrick, an American woman who harbors a secret past. Holmes needs to decipher these "dancing men" before tragedy strikes.

The Dancing Men runs July 23rd - August 16th. All shows are Thursdays to Sundays at 6:30pm. No reservations--arrive by 6:15pm for a good seat. Cushions provided. Hudson Classical Theater Company Shows are "Pay What You Can."  Audience donations help pay for artists stipends and production costs.

Directions to The Soldiers' & Sailors' Monument:
Subways: Take the 1 to West 86 or West 96, or take the 2 to West 96.  Walk west towards Riverside Park. You can enter the park at West 89th street or take the entrance at West 91st Street.  You will see the Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument.  Take the path to the right of the monument (not the one closest to the river). Walk around the path and you will be BEHIND the monument where audiences will be seated.

classes.name: Ice Cream Flavors of the Diaspora
classes.desc:
Modern ice cream production and the plants that create some of our favorite flavors are possible thanks to the brilliance of Black and Indigenous people. During this session we'll learn about the contributions of these people alongside the stories of plants like vanilla, sorghum, sorrel, and blueberries as we create ice creams infused with their flavors. If you want to develop your ice cream making skills and plant knowledge you won't wanna miss this session!

NYC Parks GreenThumb hosts this event. We are proud to be the nation's most extensive urban community gardening program, sustaining over 550 gardens throughout New York City. With GreenThumb's support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb's events are free and open to the public.

classes.desc:
City of Water Day 2026: Saturday, July 25

In place of our usual Sunday Open House, join Inwood Canoe Club on SATURDAY, JULY 25 for City of Water Day, celebrating water access for everyone across the five boroughs.

We will host a special Open House program in Inwood Hill Park, near the Columbia Boathouse–not at our own boathouse on the Hudson. This special version of Open House showcases the scenic waters of Muscota Marsh and Spuyten Duyvil Creek, not far from the club's original location over a hundred years ago.

City of Water Open House will be held from 1pm to 3pm, on Saturday July 25, in Inwood Hill Park. In-person sign-up begins at 12:15pm. First come, first served.

There will be no public programs at our boathouse that weekend. Regular Sunday Open House resumes on August 1.

Día de la Ciudad del Agua 2026: Sábado 25 de julio

En lugar de nuestra habitual Puertas Abiertas en domingo, vengan el SÁBADO 25 DE JULIO para el Día de la Ciudad del Agua, que celebra el acceso al agua para todos.

Tendremos un programa de Puertas Abiertas especial en el Parque Inwood Hill, en el embarcadero de Columbia, no en nuestro club. Esta versión especial de Puertas Abiertas muestra las pintorescas aguas de Muscota Marsh y Spuyten Duyvil Creek, no lejos del lugar original del club hace más de cien años.

El programa de Puertas Abiertas de la Ciudad del Agua comienza a la una de la tarde y termina a las tres. La inscripción en persona comienza a las 12:15 de la tarde. Por orden de llegada.

No habrá actividades públicas en nuestro club ese fin de semana. El programa de Puertas Abiertas habitual se reanuda el 1 de agosto.

classes.name: Inwood Canoe Club Sunday Open House
classes.location: Inwood Canoe Club (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc:
Join us for our free, guided 20-minute kayak trips on the Hudson River just north of the George Washington Bridge.

If you plan on paddling with us, please complete your 2026 season waiver prior to your first visit this year.

When:  Every Sunday, Memorial Day – Labor Day (May 24 – September 6, 2026).

Signup begins in person at 9 a.m. for three guided "waves" on a first come, first served basis. Once you've signed up, be sure to return to the boathouse on time or you'll forfeit your spot.

Sign up to paddle in a boat by yourself or with a friend in a tandem kayak (as available). No previous paddling experience necessary!

Wave times:

o   Wave 1: Arrive 9:45 a.m., launch 10 a.m.

o   Wave 2: Arrive 10:30 a.m., launch 10:45 a.m.

o   Wave 3: Arrive 11:15 a.m., launch 11:30 a.m.

 What:  Open House Sundays at Inwood Canoe Club

Kayak on one of our 12 SOTs (sit-on-tops) or in a tandem kayak (built for two).

Paddle with your family, friends or new acquaintances in our "canoemaran." Up to six guests + two ICC "Turtles" (aka members) will fit comfortably.

 Details:

All guests must be able to swim.

Must be a minimum of 8 years old. Guests under 18 must bring a parent or guardian. Whether a minor will be allowed to paddle with us depends on conditions that day and on the discretion of the club member running the Open House.

We supply boat, paddle and PFD (personal flotation device, aka life vest). No prior paddling experience required.

Wear shoes and clothes that can get wet.

Don't bring many valuables; you will need to leave them at the boathouse or supply your own waterproof bag for them. ICC is not liable for the loss of any valuables left in the boathouse, in a boat or on the surrounding property.

No pets at Open House.

Since Open House began in 2002, the club has offered the only walk-up kayaking program in Northern Manhattan. We generally serve over 1,000 guests every summer.

*****

Participe en una salida en kayak de 20-minutos, gratuita, guiada, en el Río Hudson justo al norte del George Washington Bridge.

Completar el formulario de exención.

CUÁNDO: Todos los domingos, desde Memorial Day (Día de los Caídos) a Labor Day (Día del Trabajo) – (24 de mayo – 6 de septiembre de 2026)

Puedes inscribirte en persona a partir de las 9 a.m. frente al club ICC para salir, por orden de llegada, en uno de los tres grupos guiados. Una vez que te hayas registrado, asegúrate de regresar a tiempo o perderás tu lugar.

Regístrate para remar en un kayak solo o con un amigo en un kayak tándem (según disponibilidad). ¡No se necesita experiencia previa de remar!!

Horarios de grupos guiados:

Grupo 1: entrada al embarcadero a las 9:45 a.m., salida al río en kayak a las 10 a.m.

Grupo 2: entrada al embarcadero a las 10:30 a.m., salida al río en kayak a las 10:45 a.m.

Grupo 3: entrada al embarcadero a las 11:15 a.m., salida al río en kayak a las 11:30 a.m.

QUÉ: Domingos: Puertas Abiertas en Inwood Canoe Club

Salidas en kayaks abiertos (SOT) individuales o tándem (dos personas)

Rema con tu familia, amigos o desconocidos en nuestro "canoemaran". Caben cómodamente hasta seis (6) invitados + dos miembros de ICC

DETALLES:

Lleve una mascarilla mientras esté en Inwood Canoe Club "boathouse"

Todos los visitantes deben poder nadar

Debe tener un mínimo de 8 años de edad. Los participantes menores de 18 años deben traer a un padre o tutor. La decisión de permitir remar con nosotros a un menor depende de las condiciones de ese día y de la discreción del socio del club que dirija la actividad de Open House

Suministramos bote, remo y PFD (chaleco salvavidas); no necesita experiencia previa

Vista calzado que se pueda mojar

No traiga muchas cosas de valor; tendrá que dejarlas en tierra o suministrar su propia bolsa impermeable para ellos. ICC no se responsabiliza por artículos perdidos o dañados en el embarcadero, en un bote o en la propiedad

Los participantes menores de 18 años deben traer a un padre o tutor. La decisión de permitir remar con nosotros a un menor depende de las condiciones de ese día y de la discreción del socio del club que dirija la actividad de Open House

No se permite traer mascotas

Desde que se inició el programa de Open House en 2002, el club ha ofrecido el único programa de kayak y canoa sin cita en el Norte de Manhattan. Por lo general, servimos a 1,000 visitantes cada verano.

classes.desc:
This It's My Park season, volunteer with The New York City Mountain Bike Association to rake, cut back thorn bushes and other overgrowth, clear natural debris, and pick up litter at Wolfe's Pond Park! Volunteers help conduct our monthly trail inspection, perform routine maintenance tasks, such as litter pickup raking, erosion control(digging) and signage maintenance.

Volunteer hours start at 9:00am rain or shine. Optional group off-road mountain bike ride at 12:30pm on the trails (The Route: mostly beginner friendly; Distance: ~7 miles). Both groups meet at the Maintenance Shop in the Main Parking lot at Wolfe's Pond Park  475 Cornelia Ave Staten Island, NY 10312.

classes.name: July Book Swap and Upcycle Paper Craft
classes.location: Greenbelt Nature Center (in Blood Root Valley)
classes.desc: The summer season is a time for sharing. Share your creative ideas, your smiles, and your generous spirit.The summer season is a time for sharing. Share your creative ideas, your smiles, and your generous spirit as New York Public Library and Greenbelt Educators host a book exchange and upcycled paper craft workshop. Take home something new to read, and, using paper and other second-hand materials, craft something that a special someone is sure to love.

classes.name: Kids In Motion: Addabbo Playground
classes.when: Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri & Sat · 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM
classes.desc:
The Kids in Motion program engages children in active, outdoors play. A NYC Parks staff member leads four to seven hours of free activities in NYC's playgrounds, including organized sports, games, fitness demos, board games, and more!

Kids in Motion is a free, drop-in program without registration. Children can head to the playground during the listed hours of operation to participate. Occasionally a Kids in Motion program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions.

Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Kids in Motion is not a childcare program and NYC parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees.

classes.name: Kids in Motion: Al Quinones Playground (Let's Make Clay Monsters)
classes.desc: Join NYC Parks and unleash your imagination–one monster at a time! Get ready for an afternoon of creativity, laughter, and a little bit of monster mischief! In this hands-on workshop, you'll learn how to sculpt your very own clay creature–whether it's cute, creepy, or completely out of this world. This event is free and open to the public.

classes.name: Kids in Motion: Al Quinones Playground (Let's Make Slime)
classes.desc: Join NYC Parks for a gooey, giggle-filled adventure for kids. Get ready for a hands-on, squishy, and colorful afternoon! classes.name: Mark Morris Dance Group Workshop
classes.desc: Join Mark Morris Dance Group for two interactive dance workshops, exploring accessible movement and wellness. Designed for those with limited mobility, this series is focused on flexibility, balance, and range of motion. No prior dance experience required—just bring your energy and favorite dance attire! All ages welcome. On Sunday, July 26, join for Move & Flow, a structured dance experience with a focus on wellness and maintaining physical health at Pier 1, Bridge View Lawn. On Sunday, September 27, join for Restorative Movement, a somatic movement class with a focus on mobility, flexibility, balance, and range of motion at Pier 6, Liberty Lawn. Space is limited; pre-registration is required for each class. Register Now! Things To Know Fitness Waivers Required Click here. You only need to complete once per season (covers all classes). Restrooms View nearby restrooms About This Event ✓ This is a free event ✓ Please note this event is subject to change due to weather or other extenuating circumstances ✓ For event announcements, subscribe to our newsletter. For time-sensitive updates, check our socials or the Park Alerts page Questions? Contact us at info@bbp.nyc. Are there public restrooms? There are lots of places to eat and drink in and near Brooklyn Bridge Park. Please browse through all the park concessions and nearby eateries. Many nearby restaurants, cafés, and bars are listed on online with the DUMBO BID, Atlantic Avenue BID, Montague Street BID, and Downtown Brooklyn.

classes.name: Martial Arts Classes at Herald Square Plaza
classes.desc:
Train in the heart of Manhattan with our martial arts classes at Herald Square. Open to all experience levels, these high-energy sessions combine Boxing, Muay Thai, and Kickboxing to help you build strength, sharpen technique, and boost confidence, all in a supportive, welcoming environment.

Whether you're looking to learn the fundamentals, improve your fitness, or challenge yourself with something new, our instructors will guide you every step of the way. Expect a dynamic mix of drills, conditioning, and technique. No experience is required.

 Bring your energy, invite a friend, and discover what you're capable of. Registration is required, walk ins are welcomed to register on-site.

classes.name: Monday Morning Pitch In to Pick Up
classes.desc:

Your efforts not only protect wildlife & the rest of the Bronx River ecosystem, but they also help us identify the sources of trash so we can stop the pollution where it starts!

classes.name: Morning Yoga at Bryant Park
classes.desc:
Everyone's favorite yoga program returns for summer 2026! From May 27 through September 16, join us for classes Tuesday mornings on the Upper Terrace, and Wednesday evenings on the lawn. Bryant Park Yoga presented by Halara is free to the public. Bring your mat and water and enjoy one hour of yoga with New York City's top instructors.

Please register for each class at our website online, or in person. Pre-registration is strongly encouraged. Those who pre-register opt in to receive updates on weather-related cancellations and other notifications. By pre-registering, you complete the waiver in advance and will receive weather-related updates.

Please present your QR code from your registration confirmation email to the Bryant Park check-in staff. Check-in locations can be found at all four corners of the lawn. Each check-in location is marked with pink barricades or a pink tent. We recommend you arrive at least 15 minutes early so you have time to check-in and roll out your mat before instruction begins.

Classes are cancelled only when it is deemed unsafe or uncomfortable for participants. Follow @bryantparknyc for day-of updates.

All Bryant Park Yoga classes are free and open to the public! All levels are welcome, thanks to the skill of our experienced teachers.

*Please note there will be NO CLASS on June 23 and June 30.

classes.name: Moves with Limón Dance at Bryant Park
classes.desc: Move to live accompaniment and experience the joy of dance with dancers from the Limón. Open to all levels and ages - no experience required! Bring friends, family, water on hot days, and enjoy dancing on the grass. Class meets on the northeast corner of the lawn.

classes.desc:

Location: Field 62 Urban Farm

classes.name: Movies Under the Stars: A Minecraft Movie
classes.desc:
Grab a blanket and come enjoy a movie in a neighborhood park or playground! This summer, NYC Parks and The Mayor's Office of Media and Entertainment will host family-friendly movie showings across the five boroughs.

Four misfits are suddenly pulled through a mysterious portal into a bizarre, cubic wonderland that thrives on imagination. To get back home, they'll have to master this world while embarking on a quest with an unexpected, expert crafter.

Movies begin at dusk. This event is FREE and open to the public. Glass bottles and alcoholic beverages are strictly prohibited. In the event of heavy rain or extreme weather, the screening may be canceled. The decision to cancel the event will be made prior to the screening and will be prominently posted on our website.

For more information visit nyc.gov/parks or call 311. Contact (212) 360-1430 or accessibility@parks.nyc.gov for more information regarding accessibility.

classes.name: Musical Theater Workshop Performance
classes.location: Herbert Von King Cultural Arts Center (in Herbert Von King Park)
classes.desc: Everyone is invited to the much-anticipated return of the Summer Theater Workshop Performance titled "Soulful Directions." This production serves as a timeless representation of harmony and healing, showcasing the talents of the SYEP participants from Henry Street Settlement.

classes.name: Nature Book Club
classes.location: West 218th Street and Indian Road (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc: Join the Urban Park Rangers for a monthly discussion of a nature-themed book. This month's book will be Inheritors of the Earth by Chris D. Thomas. Please have the book read before the meeting! Registration not required. Program will be at Nature Center.

classes.name: Nature Talks at Bryant Park Reading Room: Nature and Humanity
classes.desc:
New York City is wilder than you might think. Thousands of species of birds, mammals, insects, plants, fungi, and more are surviving, and thriving, in every part of our city. This new lecture series, featuring ecologists, naturalists, and other nature enthusiasts, will give you a new perspective on the surprisingly abundant living things all around you.

Guest speaker William Bryant Logan is an arborist and author of Oak: The Frame of Civilization; Dirt: The Ecstatic Skin of Earth; and Sprout Lands: Tending the Endless Gift of Trees.

classes.name: Nature's Workshop: Sandcastles and Beachcombing
classes.location: Entrance - Cornelia Avenue and Chester Avenue (in Wolfe's Pond Park)
classes.desc: On National Sandcastle Day, explore how sand and water work together to make strong sandcastles and try fun experiments on the beach. We'll also go beachcombing along the shoreline to look for shells, stones, and other natural treasures while learning how tides and waves shape what we find. It's a day of sandy science, shoreline discovery and hands‑on learning.

classes.name: No Excuses Circuit Training
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 25th, 2026 at 09:00 AM
classes.desc:
A high-energy beginner-friendly workout that combines strength and endurance exercises performed in timed stations with minimal rest. Participants rotate through movements targeting different muscle groups to improve fitness. Our goals are to increase stamina, build strength, and enhance overall body conditioning in an efficient, full-body training session while connecting with your community! \r
\r
Participants must sign in at the front desk and bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.

classes.name: Non-Fiction at Bryant Park Reading Room
classes.desc:
Liberation Summer: The Moment That Changed the Women's Movement and the Future of American Politics is a sweeping, definitive work of history exploring the road to the September 1968 protests of the Miss America pageant–one led by women's liberationists and the other organized by the emergent Miss Black America pageant–and the birth of a new politics of beauty. The book unfolds the full scope of this history, detailing the shocking injustices and passionate debates that led to the demonstrations, as well as the broader social and political landscape that gave rise to some of the most iconic women on both sides of the ideological spectrum. Engagingly told and meticulously researched, Liberation Summer proves how the battle for beauty's meaning has always been inextricably political, and how its enduring impact continues to shape our politics today.

Micki McElya is a professor of history at the University of Connecticut, specializing in the histories of women, gender, race, and sexuality in the United States from the Civil War to the present. Her last book, The Politics of Mourning: Death and Honor in Arlington National Cemetery, was a finalist for the Pulitzer Prize and received a number of other accolades; her 2007 book, Clinging to Mammy: The Faithful Slave in Twentieth-Century America was the cowinner of a 2007 Outstanding Book Award from the Gustavus Myers Center for the Study of Bigotry and Human Rights. McElya has written for the Washington Post, The Atlantic, and Boston Review, and her work has been featured in The New York Times, NPR, MSNBC, The Nation, Elle, and more. She is a graduate of Bryn Mawr College and New York University and is a Distinguished Lecturer for the Organization of American Historians.

classes.name: Non-Fiction at Bryant Park Reading Room
classes.desc:
In October 1964, Lady Bird Johnson made history as the first presidential spouse to take a leading role on the campaign trail–and she did it boldly, facing danger and shaking up the status quo. Aboard a custom, nineteen-car train called the Lady Bird Special, she traversed eight Southern states to support Lyndon B. Johnson's re-election bid amid heightened tensions following the passage of the Civil Rights Act. Author Shannon McKenna Schmidt recounts this daring 1,682-mile journey, highlighting Lady Bird's courageous leadership, the women who helped make this monumental undertaking possible, and how their strategy and civility impacted a pivotal moment in American history.

Shannon McKenna Schmidt is the author of You Can't Catch Us: Lady Bird Johnson's Trailblazing 1964 Campaign Train and the Women Who Rode with Her. Her other books include The First Lady of World War II: Eleanor Roosevelt's Daring Journey to the Frontlines and Back, which she previously presented at the Bryant Park Reading Room.

classes.name: NYC Parks Presents: Canvas of Color: A Tie-Dye Experience
classes.desc: Join NYC Parks for a tie-dye event at Nelson Playground. Participants are encouraged to bring a new t-shirt or clothing to transform into an amazing piece of wearable art. This event is free and open to the public!

classes.name: NYPL Storytime Under the Trees
classes.location: East Alcove Seating Area (in Washington Square Park)
classes.desc: Our friends from The New York Public Library's Hudson Branch bring the library to you. Designed for little ones, sing songs and read books together under the trees.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic: Conference House Park
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you'll learn everything you need to take on your first mile.

NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Located at the southernmost point of New York state, Conference House Park features four historic buildings that trace Staten Island's 300+ year history, breathtaking views of the Raritan Bay, extensive hiking and biking trails, and more.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic: Marine Park
classes.location: Carmine Carro Community Center (in Marine Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you'll learn everything you need to take on your first mile.

NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Marine Park serves the nearby communities of Canarsie, Flatlands, Mill Basin, Bergen Beach, and others. The course is flat and includes no sharp turns.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic: St. Marys Park
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you'll learn everything you need to take on your first mile.

NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

St. Mary's Park offers a wonderful respite from the hustle and bustle of urban life. Located in the Mott Haven neighborhood in the South Bronx, the park features rolling hills, expanses of green grass, and grand old trees. The site was once part of the estate of Jonas Bronck (1600-43), for whom the Bronx is named. It is one of the largest parks in the area with a pool, playground, basketball courts, an indoor recreation center, picnic seating areas, and more. In the winter, the hills are perfect for sledding. St. Mary's Park received $30 million from NYC's Anchor Parks initiative, which will renovate the park's ballfields, pathways, and amphitheater.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Astoria Park
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Astoria Park is home to the oldest and largest pool in New York City. The park features views of Manhattan and a newly renovated track funded by the Anchor Parks initiative.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Astoria Park Dog Days of Summer
classes.desc:
Bring your 4-legged friends to a special Open Run!

NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

classes.location: Baisley Pond Park House (in Baisley Pond Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Baisley Pond Park is located near JFK Airport in Springfield Gardens, Queens on the border of NYC and Nassau County. The park features tennis, handball, and basketball courts; peaceful, shady alcoves for picnicking; and plenty of plant and animal wildlife.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Baisley Pond Park New Runner Clinic
classes.location: Baisley Pond Park House (in Baisley Pond Park)

classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, weekly community-led, approximately 3 mile runs to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

The Open Run route offers views of the Brooklyn Bridge on the way out, the Statue of Liberty on the way back, and the Lower Manhattan skyline the whole way. Fornino is a great spot to get drinks and pizza after the run.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Canarsie Park
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Canarsie Park serves the neighborhoods of Canarsie and nearby Flatlands, Mill Basin, Marine Park, and Bergen Beach. Home to baseball fields and cricket pitches, it's bordered by the Belt Parkway and Paerdegat Basin.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Conference House Park
classes.desc:

Located at the southernmost point of New York state, Conference House Park features four historic buildings that trace Staten Island's 300+ year history, breathtaking views of the Raritan Bay, extensive hiking and biking trails, and more.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Crocheron Park
classes.location: Entrance to John Golden Park (in John Golden Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Widely known for its history with the Crocheron Family and summer house, Crocheron Park is home to running paths, baseball fields, and a 4-court tennis center.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Cunningham Park
classes.location: Cunningham Park Parking Lot (in Cunningham Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

One of the largest parks in Queens, Cunningham Park was built between 1928 and 1944 and offers culture, sports (including a large tennis facility), and nature activities and facilities.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Flushing Meadows Corona Park
classes.location: United Nations Ave South Entrance (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

One of the city's most iconic parks, Flushing Meadows Corona Park was the site of two 20th-century World's Fairs. It features historic walks, scenic trails, sports venues, and facilities for activities including soccer, baseball, tennis, volleyball, cricket, and more, as well as a recreation center, an indoor pool, lakes, trails, and many cultural and civic institutions.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Highland Park
classes.location: Elton Street and Jamaica Avenue (in Highland Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Located in the Cypress Hills section of Brooklyn, Highland Park is close to East New York and Bushwick in Brooklyn and the Woodhaven, Ridgewood, and Glendale neighborhoods in Queens. It's home to over 150 species of birds and to the Ridgewood Reservoir.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Inwood Hill Park
classes.location: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Evidence of Inwood Hill Park's formation exists as caves, valleys, and ridges left as the result of shifting glaciers. Evidence of its uninhabited state afterward remains as its forest and salt marsh (the last natural one in Manhattan), and evidence of its use by Native Americans continues to be discovered. Most of the park was largely untouched by wars and development. The park features a hiking trail and the Hudson River Bike Trail, and is one of the best places in NYC to spot a bald eagle. It also includes athletic fields, playgrounds, dog runs, and a barbecue area.

The course is three loops on hilly trails and walkways through woods and along a salt marsh.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Shore Road Park
classes.location: Lawn Near 79th Street Entrance (in Shore Park and Parkway)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Shore Road Park, located in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, is one of our longer Open Run courses. The park serves residents of Bay Ridge, Sunset Park, Dyker Heights, Fort Hamilton, and other surrounding neighborhoods.

The course is three loops alongside Shore Road with stunning views of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge.

classes.name: NYRR Open Run: Soundview Park
classes.location: Entrance - Lafayette Avenue and Morrison Avenue (in Soundview Park)
classes.desc:
NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.

Called the "Gateway to the Bronx River," 205-acre Soundview Park is located where the Bronx River opens into the East River. This urban green space is filled with grassy baseball and soccer fields, a cricket pitch, basketball courts, a running track, walking/biking paths, picnic fields, and more! With its extensive views of the water, Soundview Park celebrates the beauty of both the Bronx and East Rivers. The back part of our Open Run course has beautiful views of the Bronx River waterfront for participants to enjoy!

classes.name: On a Limb! In the Garden
classes.desc: 61 Franklin Street Garden group will be hosting a music night in the garden. Come enjoy a performance by On a Limb! All are welcome!

classes.name: One Book One Bronx book club
classes.desc: Come join the garden group at Bronx River Community Garden for a One Book One Bronx event! This is a weekly community space that sparks powerful conversations and celebrates diverse voices. Saturdays, May 23-August 8, registration required.

classes.name: Ongoing Museum Exhibit: New York's Brick & Terra Cotta Industries
classes.location: H.H. Biddle House (in Conference House Park)
classes.desc: NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts and masonry bricks that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States, and to learn about the area's rich terracotta and brick making tradition, including the famed former Atlantic Terra Cotta Company.

classes.desc:
Out Of The Box Biodiversity Upcycled Cardboard Sculpture, Raku Fired Clay, Watercolor, Pen & Ink

Note location:  Rutan-Becket House, 6 Shore Rd. Open by chance or by appointment.

classes.name: Ongoing Outdoor Museum Exhibit: Alice's Terracotta Garden
classes.location: H.H. Biddle House (in Conference House Park)
classes.desc:
Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House).

NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.

classes.name: Paddle and Pick Up at Starlight Park
classes.desc: Join the Bronx River Alliance for a Paddle and Pick Up in Starlight Park on July 25 from 1pm – 3pm, as part of our City of Water Day Celebration! This guided canoeing river clean up will allow participants to enjoy the river and protect local wildlife at the same time! Meeting location will be the Bronx River House (1490 Sheridan Boulevard, Bronx, NY 10459). Participants will be given gloves, grabbers, and trash bags or buckets, to safely remove floatable litter from the Bronx River. Minors above the age of 10 are welcome to participate, but MUST be accompanied by an adult. All minors must have an adult in their boat, and have a waiver signed by a legal guardian. Previous canoeing experience is strongly recommended for this activity. Advanced registration is required to participate. For more information, please email paddle@bronxriver.org Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Paint Studio
classes.desc: Join us for a free beginner friendly paint workshop at the Kissena Park Field House! No experience is necessary, and all materials will be provided.
