classes.name: Poetry at Bryant Park Reading Room
classes.desc:
Beth Ann Fennelly, a 2020 Academy of American Poets Laureate Fellow, was the poet laureate of Mississippi from 2016-2021 and teaches in the MFA Program at the University of Mississippi.  She's won grants and awards from the National Endowment for the Arts, the United States Artists, a Pushcart, and a Fulbright to Brazil.  Fennelly has published three books of poetry and four of prose. Heating & Cooling: 52 Micro-Memoirs (W.W. Norton) was an Atlanta Journal Constitution Best Book.  Her newest book, The Irish Goodbye: Micro-Memoirs was published by Norton in February. A contributor to The New York Times, The Washington Post, Esquire and other outlets, she lives with her husband, Tom Franklin, and their three children in Oxford, MS.

David Baker is the author many books of poetry, including Transit (2026), Whale Fall (2022), and Swift: New and Selected Poems (2019); his Never-Ending Birds was awarded the 2011 Theodore Roethke Memorial Poetry Prize. He is coeditor of Collected Poems of Stanley Plumly (2025). Baker's work appears in APR, The Atlantic, The Nation, The New Republic, The New York Times, The New Yorker, Poetry, and is included in the landmark anthology A Century of Poetry in The New Yorker.  Baker served for many years as Poetry Editor of The Kenyon Review. He is Emeritus Professor of English at Denison University and divides his time between Granville, Ohio, and Hudson, New York.

Maya C. Popa is the author of If You Love That Lady (W.W. Norton, 2026), Wound is the Origin of Wonder (W.W. Norton, 2022) and American Faith (Sarabande Books, 2019), as well as three chapbooks. She is the poetry reviews editor at Publishers Weekly and teaches poetry at NYU. Popa holds a PhD in Creative Writing from Goldsmiths, University of London; an MA from Oxford University, where she was a Clarendon Scholar; and an MFA from NYU. She is the founder of Conscious Writers Collective, a global online writing platform and community providing ongoing literary education and support outside formal academia.

Owen Lewis is the author of four collections of poetry and three chapbooks, most recently A Prayer of Six Wings, a finalist for the 2026 Patterson Book Prize. Honors include the 2024 E.E.Cummings Prize, the 2023 Guernsey International Poetry Prize, the 2023 Rumi Prize for Poetry, the International Hippocrates Prize for Poetry and Medicine, and the Jean Pedrick Chapbook Award. At Columbia University he is Professor of Psychiatry in the Department of Medical Humanities and Ethics and teaches Narrative Medicine.

classes.name: Public Gallery Tour
classes.cost: Free with admission to the grounds.
classes.desc:
Solo and group exhibitions at Glyndor Gallery explore the dynamic relationships between nature, culture, and site. When exhibitions are on view in the spring, summer, and fall, Gallery Greeters lead public exhibition tours on Thursdays and Saturdays at 2PM. Visitors can expect an in-depth look at artworks on view in Glyndor Gallery and in the Sunroom Project Space, showcasing the work of both emerging and established artists in the unique context of a public garden.

Each tour lasts approximately 45 minutes. Drop-in event; no registration required. Today's tour features current exhibitions.

Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251.

Glyndor Gallery is wheelchair-accessible. There is an accessible, ground-level entrance at the front of the building with an elevator that provides access to the gallery level. The Sunroom Project Space can be accessed with an ADA-compliant ramp. The restroom on the gallery level is all-gender and ADA-compliant.

classes.name: Puppet Mobile featuring Pinocchio

classes.name: Queens Jazz Trail Concert: Arturo O'Farrill
classes.location: George Seuffert, Sr. Bandshell (in Forest Park)
classes.desc:
Experience the vibrant legacy of jazz in the heart of Queens this summer! Inspired by the iconic Queens Jazz Trail Map, this free concert series brings the borough's rich jazz history to life in beautiful community parks–where legends like Armstrong, Coltrane, Fitzgerald, and more once called home. Presented by Kupferberg Center for the Arts, Flushing Town Hall, and Louis Armstrong House Museum in partnership with NYC Parks, the Queens Jazz Trail Concert Series honors Queens' extraordinary jazz legacy and community spirit.

Six-time Grammy-winning pianist, composer, and bandleader Arturo O'Farrill is a leading voice in Afro-Latin jazz, blending Latin rhythms, jazz, and contemporary composition with a powerful cultural and social vision. Born in Mexico City and raised in New York, he is the founder and artistic director of the Afro Latin Jazz Orchestra and Belongó, a nonprofit dedicated to the performance, education, and preservation of Afro-Latin music. Through his acclaimed recordings, collaborations, and teaching, O'Farrill continues to champion cultural identity, social justice, and the transformative power of music.

This concert will take place at Forest Park Bandshell in Woodhaven. It's free–just show up! Bring a blanket, lawn chair, picnic, friends–even your dancing shoes–and join us for an evening of live jazz in the park!

classes.name: Queens Jazz Trail Concert: John Yao & His 17-Piece Instrument
classes.location: Bowne Park Field House (in Bowne Park)
classes.desc:
Experience the vibrant legacy of jazz in the heart of Queens this summer! Inspired by the iconic Queens Jazz Trail Map, this free concert series brings the borough's rich jazz history to life in beautiful community parks–where legends like Armstrong, Coltrane, Fitzgerald, and more once called home. Presented by Kupferberg Center for the Arts, Flushing Town Hall, and Louis Armstrong House Museum in partnership with NYC Parks, the Queens Jazz Trail Concert Series honors Queens' extraordinary jazz legacy and community spirit.

Trombonist, composer, and arranger John Yao and his big band push modern big band jazz into bold new territory, blending swing, Latin, funk, and contemporary influences. Their latest release, Points in Time, celebrates Yao's 20-year career in NYC and the band's long-awaited return after a decade hiatus. A 2023 DownBeat Critics Poll Rising Star, Yao has performed with the Vanguard Jazz Orchestra and Afro Latin Jazz Orchestra, and collaborated with artists including Paquito D'Rivera and Kurt Elling.

This concert will take place at Bowne Park in Flushing. It's free–just show up! Bring a blanket, lawn chair, picnic, friends–even your dancing shoes–and join us for an evening of live jazz in the park!

classes.name: Queens Jazz Trail Concert: Wycliffe Gordon
classes.location: Beach 94th St. Amphitheater (in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk)
classes.desc:
Experience the vibrant legacy of jazz in the heart of Queens this summer! Inspired by the iconic Queens Jazz Trail Map, this free concert series brings the borough's rich jazz history to life in beautiful community parks–where legends like Armstrong, Coltrane, Fitzgerald, and more once called home. Presented by Kupferberg Center for the Arts, Flushing Town Hall, and Louis Armstrong House Museum in partnership with NYC Parks, the Queens Jazz Trail Concert Series honors Queens' extraordinary jazz legacy and community spirit.

Internationally acclaimed trombonist, composer, and educator Wycliffe Gordon is celebrated for his powerful sound, masterful plunger mute technique, and deep roots in jazz tradition. A former member of the Wynton Marsalis Septet and the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra, Gordon has appeared on more than 300 recordings and earned multiple DownBeat Critics Poll awards and a record 15 Jazz Journalists Association "Trombonist of the Year" honors. Alongside his global performing career, he continues to inspire new generations as Director of Jazz Studies at Augusta University.

This concert will take place at Rockaway Beach Park (Amphitheater) in Far Rockaways. It's free–just show up! Bring a blanket, lawn chair, picnic, friends–even your dancing shoes–and join us for an evening of live jazz in the park!

classes.name: Queens Recreation: Summer Sports Experience
classes.desc: The Summer Sports Experience season is in full swing! Join us for free sports instruction and competition. Activities include sports, games, demonstrations, and more.

classes.name: Queensboro Dance Festival: Performances at Fort Totten Park
classes.location: Bayside Historical Society (in Fort Totten Park)
classes.desc:
The Queensboro Dance Festival's 2026 Summer Tour will feature over 30 outdoor and indoor events across 15 Queens neighborhoods, including free shows, parties, and classes! Join them on July 26 at the BHS Castle for an exciting indoor dance event, featuring performances by The Kingdom Dance Co., Allpayana danza tradicional ecuatoriana, Kinding Sindaw, Flamenco Latino, Nā Pua Mai Ka Lani Nūioka, and Neela Zareen

This event is part of Bayside Historical Society's "Passport Concert Series: Bringing Cultures from Around the World to the Castle at Bayside." Music is culture expressed through sound. Travel with us to faraway lands to experience the beauty and passion of the music that is our universal language.

Admission is free, but space is limited. RSVPs are required to guarantee seating. Please RSVP at baysidehistorical.org/events-1/queensboro-dance-festival-performances-at-fort-totten-park-3.

An awards ceremony will be held at the end of August to celebrate the health and fitness achievements of swimmers who complete 25 miles or more. Visit https://www.nycgovparks.org/events/lap-swim to register.

classes.name: Reel Talks at Bryant Park: Conversation on Eric Rohmer
classes.desc:
Enjoy a conversation on film director Eric Rohmer with André Aciman, hosted by film connoisseur Scott Adlerberg.

André Aciman is the New York Times bestselling author of Call Me by Your Name, Out of Egypt, Eight White Nights, False Papers, Alibis, Harvard Square, Enigma Variations, Homo Irrealis, Roman Year, and Find Me. His most recent book is Room on the Sea (2025). He's the editor of The Proust Project and teaches comparative literature at the Graduate Center of the City University of New York. He lives with his wife in Manhattan.

Scott Adlerberg is the author of five novels, including Graveyard Love, Jack Waters, and The Screaming Child.  He has written many short stories and contributes pieces regularly to sites such as CrimeReads and Mystery Tribune.  He was born in New York City and lives in Brooklyn.

classes.name: Reel Talks at Bryant Park: The Making of Rear Window
classes.desc:
Alfred Hitchcock called his audience a race of Peeping Toms, and with Rear Window, he proved it. Drawing on the original papers of Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, and Grace Kelly, and featuring exclusive interviews with the families and collaborators of those who made the film, Jennifer O'Callaghan's book reveals the fascinating, outrageous, and deeply human story behind one of Hollywood's greatest achievements.

Jennifer O'Callaghan is the author of Rear Window: The Making of a Hitchcock Masterpiece in the Hollywood Golden Age (Kensington/PRH). A journalist and broadcaster with a background in entertainment reporting for Metro News, Shaw TV, NYC-TV, and CKUA radio, she has interviewed some of the world's biggest names, including Meryl Streep, Liza Minnelli, and Will Ferrell. Her second book, on the making of Rosemary's Baby, is forthcoming from Kensington.

Scott Adlerberg is the author of five novels, including Graveyard Love, Jack Waters, and The Screaming Child.  He has written many short stories and contributes pieces regularly to sites such as CrimeReads and Mystery Tribune.  He was born in New York City and lives in Brooklyn.

The stairs to the Field House are across the promenade at 102nd Street. In warm weather, this class meets on the patio outside the Field House.

classes.name: RTA Summer Sunset Concert Series featuring Richard Baratta
classes.desc:
Waterfront sunset concerts sponsored by the Riverside Tennis Association. No performance on July 4th. June 20 - Ron McClure & Friends June 27 - Morrisania Band Project July 11 - The Afro-LatineersJuly 18 - The Slim Kings July 25 - Richard Baratta August 1 - The BrooklynersAugust 8 - DurieuxRichard Baratta has worn many hats over the decades: master drummer, Grammy nominee, film producer, musician, but his latest headwear takes us down a nostalgic road of classic rock and soul recordings from the mid to late 1960's. Baratta returned to his original love, music, after a very successful 35-year career in the film business. With over 50 years of experience as a jazz drummer he enjoys the free flowing innovative sensibility that he applies to his work in the film industry. As 30 year member of the Directors Guild of America, as well as the Producers Guild of America, Baratta has worked on many Academy Award nominated films, including Donnie Brasco, The Wolf Of Wall Street, The Irishman, Joker, and others

Since 2020 Baratta has recorded five projects, most notably the album Music in Film featuring Grammy nominated track Chopsticks, with his current working band of Vincent Herring, Bill O'Connell, Paul Bollenback, Michael Goetz and Paul Rossman. Music In Film The Sequel, is a similar follow up, building and exploring more film compositions. Off The Charts, the fourth project was a critically acclaimed, audience favorite, looking back at some of the classic B- sides of Jazz from the 1970's, with musicians John Patitucci, Jerry Bergonzi, David Kikoski, and Paul Rossman. All three of these recording spent many weeks on Jazz Week, the definitive guide to national airplay.

classes.name: RTA Summer Sunset Concert Series featuring The Brooklyners
classes.desc:
Waterfront sunset concerts sponsored by the Riverside Tennis Association. No performance on July 4th. June 20 - Ron McClure & Friends June 27 - Morrisania Band Project July 11 - The Afro-LatineersJuly 18 - The Slim Kings July 25 - Richard Baratta August 1 - The BrooklynersAugust 8 - DurieuxThe Brooklyners is a NYC reggae band that blends Jamaican standards, French classics, and original conscious tunes into a unique tropical cocktail. The band got its name from its members' diversity and multicultural backgrounds, as well as from its home base of Brooklyn, NYC. Formed 10 years ago, the group has since played hundreds of shows in NYC and traveled up and down the East Coast.

The Brooklyners features David Langlois on vocals, percussion, and rhythm guitar; Ras Abda G on vocals and bass; Kikkah Jackson on vocals and drums; Andy Bassford on guitar; and Bryan Benhingove on keyboards, saxophone, and melodica

classes.name: Running Group
classes.location: Broadway and 251st Street (in Van Cortlandt Park)
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 24th, 2026 at 10:00 AM
classes.desc:
Runners of all experience levels join an easy-to-follow program of active running/walking intervals that gradually builds to more time running than walking.  The program also includes activities for non-running days to maximize performance and recovery. This program is designed for anyone starting their running journey, anyone with a goal to run a 5K and for anyone coming back to running after some time off.  The class is led by a running coach who provides guidance, support, and motivation to help achieve the goal.  \r
\r
Running group entrance is on Broadway and 251st Street and will meet by the Tortoise and Hare Statue.

classes.name: Salsa Social
classes.desc: Join our friends from Mi Salsa Dura Baile for an evening of Latin rhythm and dancing in Garibaldi Plaza! Each class starts off with a free lesson followed by an open dance floor.

classes.name: Salt Marsh Ecology Hike
classes.location: Salt Marsh Nature Center (in Marine Park)
classes.desc: Join the Urban Park Rangers for an interactive walk exploring how the landscape and ecology of Marine Park has been shaped by landfill. Learn about the efforts made to mitigate climate change, conserve natural resources and ecosystem services.

classes.name: Salt Marsh Saturday: City of Water Day Seining in the Harlem River
classes.location: Water's Edge Garden (in Randall's Island Park)
classes.desc:
Have you ever explored the Harlem River? Randall's Island Park Alliance will host a free seining program on City of Water Day!

Grab a pair of waders and help drag a seining net along the shore of the Harlem River to discover the amazing biodiversity in our waterways. Learn how to identify fish, crabs, and jellyfish. All equipment is provided, but wear clothes you don't mind getting wet! You must be 12 years or older to seine, but all are welcome to participate in our shoreline activities.

Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day – taking place this year on Saturday, July 25 – is a  region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York–New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP), to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor.

From 11 AM – 2 PM, our Natural Areas staff will assist you in bringing a 30-foot net into the river to find some unique species of plants and animals that call this amazing and dynamic ecosystem home!

We will also have part of our beloved Salt Marsh brought to the beach! RIPA's team will bring some of the area's wildlife and teach you about the vital ecosystem that protects nearby areas from flooding and erosion and creates habitats for countless creatures.

Location: Water's Edge Garden Beach at The Rock Dock

For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

classes.name: Screen Printing for Beginners
classes.desc: Join us for a FREE Screen Printing Workshop at the Kissena Park Field House! In this workshop, you'll learn a variety of screen printing techniques to create a custom printed design. No experience is necessary, and every one of all ages is welcome! Please bring a clean a T-shirt or a canvas tote bag to print your design on.

classes.name: Seining
classes.location: Entrance - East 88th Street and Seaview Avenue (in Canarsie Park)
classes.desc: Our experienced Urban Park Rangers teach the ecology of our waterways on every seining program. Get ready to get your hands wet; seining programs use nets to catch fish. All equipment is provided; this program is recommended for ages 8 years and older. Participation is first-come, first-served.

classes.name: Shakespeare As You Like It
classes.location: Union Turnpike and 196th Street (in Cunningham Park)
classes.desc: Free Shakespeare As You Like It performance at 7:30 p.m. Kids interaction show at 7pm, just prior to the performance.

classes.name: SharQui- The belly dance workout
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 23rd, 2026 at 06:00 PM
classes.desc: In a SharQui class, you'll learn new moves and combinations in flow. You'll gradually build up to more complex movements starting with the very basics! If you can walk in place, you can belly dance with SharQui. This class is taught to music with easy-to-follow steps that will surely get your heart pumping!

classes.name: Shea Butter Grooves
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 25th, 2026 at 12:00 PM
classes.desc:
This multi-genre dance class in celebration of joy across the African diaspora. We’ll move through the infectious rhythms of the Caribbean, the pulsing energy of Detroit-style house music, the funk and attitude of hip hop, and the feel-good grooves of reggae and R\u0026B.  \r
\r
Come ready to sweat, let loose, and connect with movement that honors the vibrancy and resilience of diaspora culture worldwide. All levels welcome—just bring an open heart and your energy!

classes.name: Shoreline Cleanup
classes.location: Inwood Canoe Club (in Inwood Hill Park)
classes.desc:
Inwood Canoe Club and Friends of Inwood Hill Park are recruiting volunteers to clean up our beloved Hudson River shoreline. We'll be collecting trash to keep our waterfront community clean. Trash bags, work gloves, latex gloves, and grabbers will be provided. Feel free to bring your own work gloves and water bottle.
Be sure to complete your 2026 season waiver before you arrive at the boathouse.

Inwood Canoe Club en asociación con Friends of Inwood Hill Park están reclutando voluntarios para limpiar nuestra querida orilla del río Hudson. Recogeremos basura para limpiar nuestra comunidad litoral. Proporcionaremos bolsas de basura, guantes de trabajo, junto con algunos guantes de látex y agarradores. Puedes traer tus propios guantes de trabajo y una botella de agua.
No olvides completar tu formulario Smart Waiver antes de venir a Inwood Canoe Club.

classes.name: Silent Disco On The Bay
classes.desc:
First Set 7:30–8:00 p.m., Second Set 8:00 p.m.–8:30 p.m.

Little Bay Park Parking Lot Under Bridge

Join Us For A Free Family-Friendly silent disco event. Bring an ID to borrow headphones. Headphone supplies are limited.

classes.name: Sips & Scholars
classes.desc: Come to Success Garden - East New York to enjoy Brooklyn Public Library's Sips & Scholars summer series, hosting free lectures in bars, cafes, and parks all over Brooklyn. Beyond offering free, high quality educational opportunities for adults, our librarians are available at each event to sign up folks for library cards, promote upcoming programs, and offer giveaways.

classes.name: Skyline CircleSing with Gaia Music Collective
classes.desc:
Join us for an evening of spontaneous harmonies, soulful songs, and playful explorations of rhythm and melody!

Led by Gaia Music Collective in partnership with Brooklyn Bridge Park, these sessions are warm, welcoming spaces for spontaneous singing and harmonizing, led with joy, passion, and kindness by skilled circlesong facilitators, taking place at Pier 3 Greenway Terrace in Brooklyn Bridge Park.

RSVPs encouraged but not required. Walk-ups welcome. More details at: brooklynbridgepark.org/event/skyline-circlesing-with-gaia-music-collective-july-29.

What is circlesinging?

A spontaneous vocal jam, sung by all of us and led by a facilitator in the middle of the circle who invents parts, teaches improvised harmonies, and shapes the music as we flow from meditative moments to vibrant dance parties to heartfelt anthems and everything in between!

classes.name: Soca Sweat Dance Fitness
classes.location: NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene- Bedford-Stuyvesant Center
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 28th, 2026 at 06:00 PM
classes.desc: This Dance Fitness class will not only provide you with a full body workout- but with fun as well! Soca Sweat will invigorate and refresh the mind, body and soul. Enjoy the music, move your body to choreography, and sweat it out!

classes.name: Soccer Skills with New York Football Academy
classes.desc: Lace up and get ready to move! Youth will meet at the farm before heading to St. Nicholas Playground North for a fun soccer skills session with NYFA. Kids will build confidence, practice teamwork, and sharpen their footwork through games, drills, and plenty of movement. Worms at Work: Compost Sifting & Soil Discovery Dates: 7/25 Get your hands in the soil and meet the tiny workers helping our farm grow! Participants will sift compost, explore worm bins, and learn how food scraps turn into rich, healthy soil. Perfect for curious kids, families, and anyone who loves a little garden science. Garden Glow-Up: Plant-Based Beauty Workshop Dates: 8/1 Discover how plants can nourish us inside and out! This hands-on workshop explores simple, plant-based beauty practices using natural ingredients inspired by the garden. Participants will learn how herbs, flowers, and everyday plants can support self-care, wellness, and creativity. Tie-Dye on the Farm Dates: 8/8 Bring your creativity and get ready for a colorful afternoon! Participants will twist, fold, and dye their own bright designs while enjoying a fun, relaxed art activity on the farm. A perfect summer workshop for families, youth, and anyone who loves wearable art. Farm & Flavor Workshops 1:30–3:00 PM | All Ages Dates: 8/15, 8/29, 9/26 Join us for Farm & Flavor, a hands-on workshop that connects what we grow to what we eat. Participants will take a sensory walk through the farm, meet the chickens, and end with a fun cooking activity that celebrates fresh flavors, culture, and community. Come ready to harvest, taste, and learn! Volunteer OpportunitiesWe welcome volunteers from our community to help with tasks on our farms! No farming skills are necessary, all you need is to bring yourself and a positive, can-do attitude.

classes.name: Spotted Lanternfly Trap Building
classes.location: Greenbelt Nature Center (in Blood Root Valley)
classes.desc: Join NYC H20 at the Greenbelt Nature Center to build traps to stop the spread of invasive spotted lanternflies.Join NYC H20 at the Greenbelt Nature Center to build traps to stop the spread of invasive spotted lanternflies. classes.name: Stargazing on the High Line
classes.location: On the High Line at 13th Street (in The High Line),under The Standard, at 13th St. (in The High Line)
classes.desc:
Immerse yourself in the wonders of the night sky every Tuesday on the High Line! The friendly team from the Amateur Astronomers Association–with whom we've been partnering since the park's opening–will be there with high-powered telescopes, ready to reveal the secrets of the cosmos.

No experience is necessary and telescopes will be provided between sunset and park closure.

This High Line program is free and open to visitors of all ages. No RSVP is required.

Stargazing on the High Line is subject to cancellation due to overcast conditions or inclement weather. If a stargazing session needs to be cancelled, we'll add an alert to the High Line's webpage.

classes.name: Step Aerobics
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 27th, 2026 at 10:30 AM
classes.desc:
Step Aerobics is a cardio workout that will elevate your heart rate, strengthen muscles, and improve coordination and balance.  \r
\r
Please register for updates about cancellations. Class is first come, first served, but for safety reasons, participants will not be allowed to join after the first fifteen minutes of class. New Settlement Community Center requires all first-time participants to create a profile with the site upon arrival so please arrive at least 15 minutes in advance.

classes.name: Stewardship Morning at the Foodway
classes.desc: Lend a hand and help steward the Bronx River Foodway. Help with tasks like weeding, laying mulch, and planting some edible and native at the Foodway. This is a reoccurring event, join us Tuesday mornings through October 2026.

classes.name: StoryTime at Bryant Park Reading Room
classes.desc:
Get ready to sing, clap, and stomp your feet with Cali Co Cat and friends! Our free children's storytime events bring imaginative stories and the magic of live "Meowsic" to kids ages 2–8.

Produced in partnership with Penguin Random House.
*Free books while supply lasts*

classes.desc:

classes.name: Strength & Cardio Circuit Training
classes.desc: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full body workout. Whether you are a beginneror looking for a challenge; this class for all fitness levels and modifications are always provided. Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups, get your heart pumping, and connect with people in your community!

classes.name: Strength In Motion
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 23rd, 2026 at 01:00 PM
classes.desc: A fun and inclusive bodyweight strength training class where we focus on stretching, mobility, and building strength without weights. Designed for everybody and every fitness level. Come move, feel stronger, and grow at your own pace.

classes.name: Strong & Balanced Circuit Training
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on August 5th, 2026 at 05:45 PM
classes.desc:
This low-impact bodyweight circuit training class focuses on building strength, endurance, balance, and functional fitness through a variety of full-body exercises. Participants will improve overall conditioning while developing the core stability and balance needed for everyday activities and long-term health. All levels are welcome in this beginner-friendly class!\r
\r
Please bring a yoga mat and water.

classes.name: Strong and Steady Circuit Training
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 27th, 2026 at 06:00 PM
classes.desc: Strong \u0026 Steady Circuit Training is a full-body workout designed for high-energy fun. This class focuses on the mind-body connection, combining steady, controlled movements with high-energy sequences to build strength. Come ready to move, breathe, and leave feeling energized.

classes.name: Strong For Life Circuit Training
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on August 1st, 2026 at 10:00 AM
classes.desc: A welcoming exercise class focused on helping participants feel stronger, more mobile, and more energized in daily life. Class includes low-impact strength and endurance exercises, mobility training, functional fitness and balance work. Modifications will be provided for all levels of fitness.

classes.name: STRONG Nation®
classes.location: Dance Room (in Constance Baker Motley Recreation Center)
classes.regDeadline: Registration ends on July 25th, 2026 at 01:00 PM
classes.desc:
STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. Mats are available for those who need one. \r
\r
Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.

classes.name: STRONG Nation®
classes.location: Rock Climbing Wall Room (in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center)

classes.desc:
STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more.  \r

classes.name: Summer Concert Series -  The Zoo
classes.location: George Seuffert, Sr. Bandshell (in Forest Park)
classes.desc: Put your dancing shoes on and feel the electrifying energy of MTV's Reality Show winners, The Zoo, as they perform classic and current country songs from Johnny Cash, Zac Brown, Dolly Parton, Shania Twain and more!

classes.name: Summer Concert Series - Queens Jazz Trail - Arturo O'Farrill
classes.location: George Seuffert, Sr. Bandshell (in Forest Park)
classes.desc: Experience a musical journey through the borough's rich jazz heritage where legends like Louis Armstrong, John Coltrane, Dizzy Gillespie, Ella Fitzgerald, Count Basie, Tony Bennett, Lena Horne, and scores of others once called home.  Enjoy the sounds of six-time Grammy-winning pianist, composer, and bandleader Arturo O'Farrill, a leading voice in Afro-Latin jazz and contemporary composition with a powerful cultural and social vision.

classes.name: Summer Concert Series - Queensboro Dance Festival
classes.location: George Seuffert, Sr. Bandshell (in Forest Park)
classes.desc: Enjoy performances by Queens-based professional dance companies featuring diverse styles.  'Danceback' and try a few dance moves, then party to a live Queens band.

classes.name: Summer Movies in the Park | Shrek 2
classes.desc:
Enjoy this beloved animated feature starring Shrek and his love, Fiona! Now married, they travel to Fiona's kingdom, Far Far Away.

Prospect Park Alliance, Fort Greene Park Conservancy, and Rooftop Films announce Summer Movies in the Park, presented by NewYork-Presbyterian. This free, outdoor movie series in Prospect Park and Fort Greene Park takes place on Wednesday nights this summer, with screenings on August 5th and August 12th in Prospect Park, and August 26th and September 9th in Fort Greene Park.

August 5: Shrek 2

6:00 PM: Lawn Opens
8:30 PM: Film Begins

Directions: Enter Prospect Park at Grand Army Plaza to access the north end of the Long Meadow

Shrek 2 (PG)
Andrew Adamson | 2004 | US | 92 mins
Enjoy this beloved animated feature starring Shrek and his love, Fiona! Now married, they travel to Fiona's kingdom, Far Far Away. The catch: her parents are unaware of the curse that struck their daughter and have assumed she married Prince Charming, not a 700-pound ogre with a talking donkey pal.

The films will begin shortly after sundown; come early to picnic and enjoy pre-movie entertainment. Please note that the movie may be cancelled in the case of inclement weather. Attendees will be notified via Eventbrite and the Prospect Park and Fort Greene Park social media channels.

This series is presented through support of NewYork-Presbyterian.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Amplified Sundays featuring Chispa
classes.desc:
Live music accompanied by spectacular sunsets over the Hudson River. July 5: Wayne Tucker and the Bad Mothas | Energetic, improvisational jazz
 July 12: Gentleman Brawlers | Psychedelic Afro-soul revival and indie dance
 July 19: Dawn Drake and ZapOte | Genre-defying pop, funk, and groove
 July 26: Chispa | Love songs for the land rooted in the musical traditions of Latin AmericaChispa is a music project dreaming of composting systems of oppression, reconnecting with the land, and channeling the power of queer love. Rooted in musical traditions of Latin America and the Caribbean, such as Cumbia and Bolero, the project is led by vocalist and songwriter Julia Rocha-Nava, alongside Sebastián Ángel-Romero (Guitar), Mobéy Lola Irizarry (Congas), lee hooper (Flute), Jamila Ravel (Bass) and Daniela Serna (Percussion).

Chispa's debut album, "Somos Medicina", is a collection of queer love songs for the land. The project has received support from the New Music USA Creator Fund and the Brooklyn Arts Council's Creative Equations Grant for Justice, Sustainability, and Equity and the 2025 Performing Artist in Residence at the Brooklyn Botanic Garden. Chispa is known for live performances that are both energetic and tender–inviting audiences to dance, sing, and heal together through songs that celebrate friendship, seed saving, and collective liberation.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Art in the Park
classes.desc:
Art in the Park invites children ages 3-8 to learn about their local environment through a mix of arts & crafts activities. This hands-on, place-based curriculum builds kids' appreciation for the plants, pollinators, and wildlife found in Riverside Park, and fosters their creativity.

Mondays and Thursdays at Riverbank (Camel) Playground; Tuesdays and Wednesdays at Hippo Playground. Art in the Park is fully funded by proceeds from the Hippo Playground Spring Fair.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Art in the Park
classes.desc:
Art in the Park invites children ages 3-8 to learn about their local environment through a mix of arts & crafts activities. This hands-on, place-based curriculum builds kids' appreciation for the plants, pollinators, and wildlife found in Riverside Park, and fosters their creativity.

Mondays and Thursdays at Camel Playground; Tuesdays and Wednesdays at Hippo Playground. Art in the Park is fully funded by proceeds from the Hippo Playground Spring Fair.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Ballroom Basix
classes.location: On the Pier (in West Harlem Piers)
classes.desc:
Join Summer on the Hudson and Ballroom Basix USA  for fitness, fun, and fancy footwork! Ballroom Basix provides physical, social, emotional and multicultural engagement through the etiquette and education of non-competitive Ballroom & Latin partner dancing. This class energizes, entertains and educates teachers, students, their families & communities while emphasizing the poise & politeness our program promotes, true to our meaningful motto: "Making manners matter...every move we make!"

About Ballroom Basix: The BBX syllabus and methodology creatively contribute to the health and wellness of the diverse schoolchildren whom we serve by getting classmates hand-in-hand, arm-in-arm and eye-to-eye. The partner dancing experience is enhanced by the verbal & physical courtesy and consent inherent to partner dancing as students dance – rotationally & respectfully – with one another.

About the teaching artist: Marisa Caputo is a recent graduate of NYU Tisch School of the Arts where , in addition to her bachelors degree studies, she was active in NYU's Latin & Ballroom Dance Team. She trained in partner dancing for multiple years of through the Fred Astaire Dance Studios, where she has also taught part time. She has been a valued Teaching Artist with BALLROOM BASIX for almost 4 years – and is thrilled to share her love of Ballroom & Latin dance with children, young adults, and seniors throughout New York City.

Marisa continues to train and compete in all four ballroom styles–Latin, Rhythm, Standard, and Smooth–and is passionate about creating a fun, welcoming environment for dancers of all ages and experience levels. She is very excited to be teaching this summer at Riverside Park!

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Children's Performance Series featuring Arm-of-the-Sea Theatre
classes.desc:
Music, storytelling, and theatre for young audiences up to age 7. Wear sunscreen. July 9: Musiquita | Joyful bilingual music for kids inspired by Latin folk traditions
 July 16: Lucy Kalantari & the Jazz Cats | Interactive swingin' sounds and jazz age play
 July 23: Joanie Leeds | GRAMMY-winning singer-songwriter
 July 30: Arm of the Sea Theatre | presents "One Blue Sky"Arm-of-the-Sea is an arts ensemble that fuses visual storytelling with live music in large-scale works of mask and puppet theater. Founded in 1982 by Marlena Marallo and Patrick Wadden, the company's hybrid performance style channels the evocative power of the arts as a force for joy, insight, and community uplift. Their low-tech devices of theatrical enchantment illuminate relations between humans and the life-support networks of this blue-ocean planet. Under the direction of Marallo & Wadden, the creative team includes musicians Maclain Maier, Joseph Davis & Helen Houghton; and performers Joanna Grabiarz, Doug Keller, DeeDee Maucher, Nick Panken, Rakel Stammer, Soyal Smalls and MJ Smythe.

Arm-of-the-Sea's ONE BLUE SKY combines live music, puppet figures and visual poetry in a contemporary work of mask & puppet theater.  Mareesha is a young girl who undertakes a mythic journey to find her lost parent and discover her own powers. It is appropriate for ages 6 & up. Arm-of-the-Sea is an award-winning theater company currently in its 44th season. This production was made possible, in part, by a grant from the Jim Henson Foundation.

Arm-of-the-Sea tours its original productions to cultural centers, festivals, and community venues throughout New York and adjacent states. The organization is transforming the ruins of a 19th century papermill In Saugerties, NY into the Tidewater Center, a waterfront cultural park for bio-regional arts, environmental science & local heritage.Recent recognitions include: 2024 Hudson River Watershed Alliance "Wave-Makers" Award, 2018 Ulster County Executive's Arts Award for Arts Organization, a 2016 Environmental Champions Award from the US Environmental Protection Agency, the 2014 Excellence in Arts Award from Mill Street Loft, and the 2014 Distinguished
Environmental Achievement Award from Mohonk Consultations. Directors Marallo & Wadden were 2013 Artists-in Residence at SUNY Ulster (Stone Ridge, NY), and 2014 Artists-in-Residence at the Cary Institute for Ecosystem Studies (Millbrook, NY).

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Children's Performance Series featuring Joanie Leeds
classes.desc:
Music, storytelling, and theater for young audiences up to age 7. Wear sunscreen. July 2: Arm of the Sea Theatre | "The Life and Times of the Hudson River"
 July 9: Musiquita | Joyful bilingual music for kids inspired by Latin folk traditions
 July 16: Lucy Kalantari & the Jazz Cats | Interactive swingin' sounds and jazz age play
 July 23: Joanie Leeds | GRAMMY-winning singer-songwriterGRAMMY Winning, NYC based singer-songwriter Joanie Leeds is a National touring artist, early childhood music educator, host of JKids Radio Show, Holiday Hang, proud Recording Academy member and Governor of the NY Chapter.

For her original children's music, she has won 1st place in the USA Songwriting Competition, Independent Music Awards, The International Songwriting Competition, Gold Parents' Choice Awards, NAPPA Gold Awards, Family Choice Awards and is a John Lennon Songwriting Award Finalist. Joanie has played at Lollapalooza, Austin City Limits, Clearwater Festival, The Kennedy Center & Lincoln Center, Wolftrap, Levitt Pavilion as well Limmud UK, URJ's Biennial, Hootenanny NYC and dozens of JCCs, Synagogues and Temples Nationwide. Her 12 albums of Jewish and secular music have been featured in The New York Times, People Magazine, Parents Magazine, Billboard, Huffington Post, NPR, The Washington Post, Times of Israel and KVELLER.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Friday Night Jams featuring Christine Chanel
classes.location: 125th Street and Marginal Street (in West Harlem Piers)
classes.desc:
Get out to the park and kick off the weekend with some local sounds at this monthly musical get-happy hour. Friday Night Jams takes place on the last Friday of the month from May through August.May 29 - Wes D'Alelio / original songwriting and fingerstyle guitarJune 26 - Ramio Marziani  / improvisational jazz and grooveJuly 31 - Christina Chanel / powerful vocals and indie - soulAug 28 - Ilana Morris / classic R&B with modern edgeChristine Chanel is an indie-soul recording artist based in Westchester, NY. Known for her powerful yet intimate vocals and emotionally grounded songwriting, her music blends soul and indie influences with the patience of an artist who lets songs grow through live performance.

A fixture of the New York tri-state music scene, Christine has spent years connecting with audiences in rooms both large and small, often alongside an incredible roster of musicians. Her approach is intentional and unforced, focused on creating moments that feel real, resonant, and lasting.

classes.name: Summer on the Hudson: Mycelium - A Solar-Powered Concert
classes.desc:
RESCHEDULED from Thursday, 7/16!

Mycelium: A Solar-Powered Orchestral Avant-Pop Concert About Hidden Worlds

Inspired by the hidden intelligence of fungi and plankton, this musical suite weaves orchestral textures with modern jazz/pop influences to show how even the smallest forms of life can help us understand ourselves and connect more deeply with each other.

About the Artist: Praised for her depth of dimension, multimodal artist Sirintip Phasuk draws inspiration from a range of styles and disciplines. Her works explore unsung intersections of humankind and the natural world – a focus that drives her curiosity and expands her output. Part ethereal, part impassioned, her vocals move nimbly across sophisticated harmony, soar over dense walls of sound, and pulse through rhythmic modulations. A highly sought studio collaborator, Sirintip has shared her unique sound on more than 30 releases.

classes.name: Summer Sports Experience: Basketball: Skills and Drills
classes.when: Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 10:00 AM - 1:00 PM
classes.desc:
Summer Sports Experience is an instructional youth program that teaches sports skills. Our knowledgeable NYC Parks staff train kids in sports such as basketball, ball hockey, kickball, flag football, pickleball, soccer, team handball, track and field, and volleyball. This program covers basic and more advanced techniques.

Summer Sports Experience is a drop-in program recommended for children ages 8-14. Participants can stop by for one or more sessions, but kids who stay for the whole program will get the most benefit from it. Occasionally a Summer Sports Experience program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions.

Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Summer Sports Experience is not a childcare program, and NYC Parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees.

For more information, please visit nyc.gov/parks.

Contact accessibility@parks.nyc.gov or (212) 360-1430 for more information regarding accessibility.

classes.name: Sunset Wednesdays 2026
classes.cost: Premium admission 4–8PM: $15/adults; $10/students/seniors; $8/children 6-18; FREE/members and children under 6
classes.desc:
Named by Time Out as one of the top "10 under-the-radar cultural experiences in NYC," Wave Hill's annual summer tradition brings five exciting performances Wednesday evenings to our Great Lawn. Pack a picnic and bring your favorite blanket or chair for an unforgettable summer evening as the sun sets over the Hudson River.

July 8
Jennifer Jade Ledesna Bronx-born and raised, multilingual jazz vocalist

July 15
Melissa Aldana Chilean-born tenor saxophonist

July 22
Jake Blount award-winning scholar and string band performer of Black folk music

July 29
Stella Cole vocalist delivering stunning interpretations from the Great American Songbook

August 5
Djékady Trio combining Malian music with American folk and contemporary styles

Sunset Wednesdays take place on the Great Lawn. Visitors may bring folding chairs and blankets. Picnicking is permitted on the Great Lawn beginning at 4PM. Glyndor Gallery, The Café and Shop are open until 7:30PM.

Reservations are not required for the Great Lawn. In the event of inclement weather, this program will move indoors where capacity is limited. Pre-registered Rain or Shine tickets receive priority seating indoors and must be shown at the Front Gate at time of admission. Same-day, indoor tickets based on availability.

Premium admission is in effect from 4PM to 8PM each Sunset Wednesday. Wave Hill Member-for-a-Day and Member Guest passes are accepted these evenings. All other discount passes will not be accepted starting at 4PM.

Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251.

classes.name: Tapestry Weaving Workshop with Amirtha Arasu
classes.desc: Join Amirtha for a workshop that aims to teach the basics of tapestry weaving and how to use a simple frame loom. Participants will be introduced to different materials and will be able to take a hand-made loom home after the workshop, so you can keep on weaving! Fibers will also be provided. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Tea and Meditation with Tia Colon
classes.desc: Join us on The Bronx River with Meditation Facilitator Mattia "Tia" Colón MSW, in this immersive sound bath meditation + herbal tea tasting which invites participants (kids, caregivers, elders, everyone!) into a sacred space of calmness and tranquility. Through the soothing sounds and gentle vibrations of Tibetan meditation instruments, we'll open pathways for healing, stress relief and focus. After the meditation, we'll gather together for an herbal tea tasting and explore the herbs growing on the Bronx River Foodway. All ages welcome! Bring your family and friends to connect with meditation and locally grown herbs. If called please bring a journal to take notes and/or write down your thoughts. Note: This is one of this year's Community Grant workshops as well as a part of a larger City of Water Day Celebration supported by the Bronx River Alliance. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.desc: Teach a Horse to Fish is a program that introduces Bronx residents to their natural environment through Fishing. The goal of the program is to stir interest in the environment and learn sustainable fishing. Informational zines will be distributed along with program. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Team RIVER Volunteers
classes.desc:
Volunteer with Team RIVER (Randall's Island Volunteer Ecological Restorers) and help keep Randall's Island Park beautiful! Team RIVER Volunteers regularly work with the Natural Areas team twice a month to take care of and restore our natural spaces on the island. With our Horticulture team, volunteers care for the many gardens, green streets and meadows, every second Wednesday of each month. Team RIVER also works with our Sports Field and Operations teams, keeping our park clean and maintained. If you are only able to volunteer on a limited basis, you are still very much welcome!

This volunteer opportunity is open to individuals from the community. Groups of more than 5 people must contact: Volunteers@RandallsIsland.org

Volunteers must be at least 11 years old. Anyone under the age of 18 must have a waiver signed by their legal guardian before starting any work. Children under 16 must also have an adult with them while volunteering. A downloadable volunteer waiver can be found at this link: General Volunteer – Randall's Island

Registration is required here.

Location: Volunteers will be notified after registration.

For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation, or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

classes.name: The Bronx Museum Presents: Meet Me At The Park–Wood Carving With Artist Jill Cohen-Nuñez

For this iteration of Meet Me At The Park, artist Jill Cohen-Nuñez is leading a wood-carving workshop. Join in and learn about wood identification and safe wood carving techniques.  More info at bronxmuseum.org/park-cohen-nunez.

classes.name: The Light That Shines Through the Universe: The Nature of Light
classes.location: On the High Line at 30th St. (in The High Line),The Spur (in The High Line)
classes.desc:
Featuring a lecture by Donald S. Lopez, a distinguished university professor of Buddhist and Tibetan Studies at the University of Michigan. This session delves deeper into the name of the artwork The Light That Shines Through The Universe, and explores light in Buddhist thought–both literal and metaphorical.

The lecture will be followed by a guided meditation by Jungwon Kim, a Zen practitioner of the Plum Village Tradition and the facilitator for Love Circle, a Brooklyn-based BIPOC sangha.

Rain date: August 6, 12:30 – 1:30pm

Modifications are always offered, making this class great for all fitness levels. We recommend that you bring a towel, water bottle, and yoga mat.

classes.name: Trail Work Thursdays
classes.location: VCPA Learning Garden (in Van Cortlandt Park)
classes.desc: Be a part of the community effort to maintain the trails in Van Cortlandt Park.Note: Thursday 7/2 has been canceled due to extreme heat. Meeting Details: Location: VCPA Learning Garden Across from the Riverdale Stables Time: 9 am sharp; work begins at 9:15 am Upcoming Activities Include: Clearing debris and overgrowth Ensuring trail safety and accessibility Maintenance on structures that mitigate erosion and improve access. What to Bring: Comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing Closed-toed shoes Water and a snack Why Join Us? Contribute to the vitality and safety of the trails you love! Engage with fellow community members, learn about trail maintenance, and enjoy the satisfaction of hands-on work in the great outdoors.Necessary tools and gloves will be provided! Want to bring a group? This event is limited to small groups of 5 or less. If you would like to bring more 6 or more people, please reach out to garcialeslie@vancortlandt.org to schedule a separate event. Volunteer Policy for Minors • Ages 15 – 17 A parent or guardian must sign and submit a volunteer waiver. Once the waiver is on file, the minor may participate without an accompanying adult. • Ages 12 – 14 A parent or guardian must accompany the minor and remain on-site for the entire project. • Under 12 Children may volunteer only at events specifically labeled family-friendly and must be accompanied by a parent or guardian at all times.Read moreGood to knowHighlightsIn personRefund PolicyRefunds up to 7 days before eventHighlightsIn personRefund Policy Refunds up to 7 days before eventLocationVCPA Learning GardenBroadway & Mosholu Ave.

classes.name: Trees Count at Asser Levy Park
classes.location: Asser Levy Park Play Area (in Asser Levy Park)
classes.desc: Volunteer with NYC Parks for Trees Count 2025! At each event you will receive a tote bag with all the materials needed to count trees. Next, we'll provide a 30-minute training session that includes the basics of tree identification and how to measure and assess each tree. Using our Tree Census app, you'll split up into different zones within the park to start counting trees! After completing your first training event, you are welcome to continue counting trees on your own in smaller parks across the city. For your comfort and safety, please bring water and wear closed-toe shoes to this program.

classes.name: Turtle Quest: Explore, Observe, Protect with Lizbeth Miron
classes.desc: Join Lizbeth for an exploration of the freshwater turtles that reside in the Bronx River in Shoelace Park, while learning about local ecology and conservation. We will learn to identify native and non-native turtle species and use iNaturalist, a community science app, to document wildlife observations. This workshop also includes a park clean-up to help protect turtle habitats and support the health of the Bronx River ecosystem. The goals of the workshop are to increase awareness of urban wildlife, encourage community involvement in conservation, and empower participants to contribute to environmental research and stewardship in their local parks. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Urban Farm Volunteers
classes.desc:
Volunteer with our team to manage our one-acre Urban Farm!

Whether you are new to our farm or have been a tried-and-true weekly volunteer, come and join us! Our farmers have a lot of farm chores and composting to do weekly and you can help us take care of this important urban educational agricultural resource.

On Sundays we not only do farm chores, but we are joined by the NYC Compost Project with Big Reuse who help us manage our 5-bin compost system to create much needed organic matter for our planting beds!

Individuals under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an adult who volunteers with them. Individuals ages 16 & 17 can volunteer if a parent or guardian has signed a waiver. Please find a downloadable waiver here: Waiver for unaccompanied 16 & 17 year olds

This volunteer event is for individuals from the community and not open to groups larger than 5 people. If you wish to bring more than 5 people, you must first contact us at volunteers@randallsisland.org.

New volunteers must register for this event here.

Location: Urban Farm

For inquiries about accessibility, to request accommodation, or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

classes.name: Volunteer Day: Bay 84th Street Community Garden
classes.desc:
GreenThumb community gardens are developed, led, and cared for by dedicated volunteer gardeners who critically upkeep them as public green spaces.

Bay 84th Street Community Garden, is searching for its community! We invite you to come visit the garden and help reimagine what this vital green space can be. We will be hosting an open garden day and volunteer session. Join us for hands-on community service open to all levels to help care for the garden, along with fun garden activities. We will have a kids' art station and teach visitors how to make their own smudge sticks to take home, using the same plant we will be weeding. This garden is creating space for those who are curious and willing to dig in. Join us! No prior experience necessary - beginners are welcome! GreenThumb will provide supplies and facilitation.

This is a great opportunity to support and join a dynamic community space while learning about community gardens. Please wear comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing and bring water and snacks. Note: Some tasks may involve lifting heavy materials and extended periods of bending, squatting, and moving.

Register here!

Join our second event on August 15th here!

Location: 3-22 Beach 84th St, Rockaway Beach, NY 11693

For information about group volunteering, please contact Volunteer Program Coordinator, Olivia Navarro at Olivia.Navarro@parks.nyc.gov

This event is co-hosted by NYC Parks GreenThumb. We are proud to be the nation's largest urban community gardening program, sustaining over 550 gardens throughout New York City. With GreenThumb's support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb's events are free and open to the public.

classes.desc:
GreenThumb community gardens are developed, led, and cared for by dedicated volunteer gardeners who critically maintain them as public green spaces.

Newport Garden is seeking volunteers and new garden members who are interested in helping preserve this vital green space. The garden is creating space for those who are curious and willing to dig in. Join us for a hands-on volunteer day to help beautify the space and spruce up the rain gardens. No prior experience necessary - beginners are welcome! GreenThumb will provide supplies and facilitation.

This is a great opportunity to support a dynamic community space while learning about community gardens and rain gardens. Please wear comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing and bring water and snacks. Note: Some tasks may involve lifting heavy materials and extended periods of bending, squatting, and moving.

Location: 195 Newport St, Brooklyn, NY 11212

With GreenThumb's support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb's events are free and open to the public.

classes.desc:
GreenThumb community gardens are developed, led, and cared for by dedicated volunteer gardeners who critically upkeep them as public green spaces.

Hattie Carthan Herban Farm is hosting bi-weekly volunteer work and learn days to engage new volunteers and gardeners with the space! The garden is creating space for those who are curious and willing to dig in. Join us for a hands-on Work and Learn Day at the Hattie Carthan Herban Farm!

Harvest beneficial herbs, and more, in a hands-on educational hour with Farmer Yon after the work party! We meet every other Friday, 4-7 p.m.

Work party tasks include weeding, pruning, mulching, and general garden clean-up. Light refreshments and all tools, including gloves, are provided.

No prior experience necessary - beginners are welcome! GreenThumb will provide supplies and facilitation.

Register here!

classes.name: Waterfront Concert Series: Forever Ray
classes.desc: Join us for concerts on the waterfront! Taking place on the Great Lawn at Astoria Park, a performance by Forever Ray. A tribute band to Ray Charles, with a range of genres from jazz, blues, country, and R&B. We hope to see you there!

classes.name: Waterfront Concert Series: StrangeMagic
classes.desc: Join us for concerts on the waterfront! Taking place on the Great Lawn at Astoria Park, a performance by StrangeMagic. A tribute band to Electric Light Orchestra and 70's pop. We hope to see you there!

classes.name: Waterfront Walks
classes.desc:
Join a free walking tour to learn about the history of the Brooklyn waterfront at Brooklyn Bridge Park! Learn about the local ecology, Brooklyn Bridge Park's sustainable design, and how the park came to life! Tours last roughly 60-90 minutes, starting from the entrance of Pier 1 and finishing near Pier 3.

Advance registration is required. Registration opens a month in advance of each date. Please note this event is subject to change due to weather or other extenuating circumstances.

classes.name: Waterfront Workouts: Amp'd Bootcamp
classes.location: Pier 2 (in Brooklyn Bridge Park),Brooklyn Bridge Park
classes.desc:
Push yourself with this free Amp'd Bootcamp class Brooklyn Bridge Park! This all-level one hour fitness class is open to all ages and abilities, led by instructors from Chelsea Piers Fitness. This is a part of Brooklyn Bridge Park's Waterfront Workouts series, offering free weekly workouts from May through September.

Designed to diversify your workout, this bootcamp-style high intensity interval training (HIIT) class will incinerate calories and build strength. Come give your all at Brooklyn Bridge Park in this all-level cardio, strength, and plyometric-based workout, with a stunning waterfront view.

No equipment is required but participants are encouraged to bring their own yoga mats or towels, if desired, to use on the turf.

Advance registration is highly encouraged but walk-ups welcome as capacity allows. Fitness waivers are required by all participants. View all upcoming dates and full details at brooklynbridgepark.org. Please note holiday weekends are skipped and this event is subject to change due to weather or other extenuating circumstances.

classes.name: West Harlem Summer Jazz Festival
classes.location: James Baldwin Lawn (in St. Nicholas Park),135th Street and St. Nicholas Avenue (in St. Nicholas Park)
classes.desc:
Join West Harlem Arts Alliance, Harlem Late Night Jazz and NYC Parks for the annual jazz festival.

The Jazz Festival takes place in Morningside, St. Nicholas, Jackie Robinson from May to September. In Montefiore Square Park, the jazz festival is from June - August, weekly on Saturdays at 6 pm. In the Historic Harlem Parks of Morningside, St. Nicholas and Jackie Robinson, there are monthly jazz events.

Morningside Park is typically second Saturdays from 5-6:30 pm at 113 Street and Morningside Ave, within the park.

St. Nicholas is typically on fourth Fridays from 6-7:30 pm at the James Baldwin Lawn and 135 Street Plaza located at 135 Street and St. Nicholas Ave.

Jackie Robinson Park is typically on first Fridays from 6-7:30 pm at the Bandshell, located at 148 Street and Bradhurst Ave. ADA access is located at 150 Street and Bradhurst Ave.

Visit whaanyc.org for the full schedule of events and artist line up.

July 24 at St. Nicholas Park will features  Kevin Oliver, Jr. Group.

classes.name: Wheel Fun Rentals Celebrates America's 250th with Patriotic LED Bike Ride
classes.location: Meadow Lake North (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park),Sand Lane and Father Capadanno Boulevard (in Franklin D. Roosevelt Boardwalk and Beach),Marine Park,Bensonhurst Park,David Dinkins Circle (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park)
classes.cost:
$29 for a Single Surrey (seats up to 3 adults and 2
small children), and $39 for a Double Surrey (seats up to 6 adults and 2 small children)
classes.desc:
Wheel Fun Rentals at Boathouse Row is lighting up summer nights in patriotic style as it celebrates America's 250th anniversary with special Red, White & Blue LED Illuminated Surrey Night Rides. Patriotic Surrey Nights Rides are available now and will run through Labor Day Weekend at their Bensonhurst Park and FDR Boardwalk at Sand Lane bike rental locations.

For the summer-long celebration, Wheel Fun Rentals has transformed its popular LED-illuminated specialty bikes into dazzling patriotic attractions featuring custom red, white, and blue lighting. Families, couples, and friends can cruise along two of New York City's scenic waterfronts while celebrating America's historic 250th birthday.

On July 4th, guests can make the holiday even more memorable by riding to waterfront viewing areas for spectacular fireworks displays. Launching from Bensonhurst Park in Brooklyn, riders can enjoy the festive atmosphere along the waterfront bike path with views across New York Harbor. On Staten Island, guests can pedal along the FDR Boardwalk, where the waterfront provides a scenic setting to take in the evening's Independence Day celebrations and fireworks over the harbor.

Night Rides are first-come, first-served and available Friday – Sundays and holidays beginning at sunset until 10pm, last 1-hour rental goes out at 9pm. Pricing is $29 for a Single Surrey (seats up to 3 adults and 2 small children), and $39 for a Double Surrey (seats up to 6 adults and 2 small children).

For additional location information, current hours of operation, and to learn more about Wheel Fun Rentals available recreational rental options, visit wheelfunrentals.com/NYC.

classes.name: Wild Apothecary: From Soil to Skin with Imelda Bell
classes.desc: Join local herbalist and forager Imelda Bell for a 2 hour hands-on community workshop that participants to explore the deep connection between local wild plants and natural skincare. The experience blends guided foraging, botanical education, and holistic skincare formulation to help participants understand how everyday “weeds” and native plants can become powerful tools for skin health and self-care. The workshop begins with a guided introduction to ethical and safe urban foraging practices, where participants will learn to identify common local plants known for their skin-supportive properties such as plantain, dandelion, yarrow, and calendula (depending on seasonal availability). During this portion, participants will also be introduced to the concept of the “wild apothecary,” learning how traditional herbal knowledge and modern skincare practices intersect. Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops. For questions or concerns, email Nathan.Hunter@parks.nyc.gov or call 718-542-4124. Sign Up Now! Stay up to date with our e-newsletter, The Current! The Bronx River Alliance serves as a coordinated voice for the river and works in harmonious partnership to protect, improve and restore the Bronx River corridor so that it can be a healthy ecological, recreational, educational and economic resource for the communities through which the river flows.

classes.name: Worms at Work: Compost Sifting & Soil Discovery
classes.desc:
Get your hands in the soil and meet the tiny workers helping our farm grow! Participants will sift compost, explore worm bins, and learn how food scraps turn into rich, healthy soil. Perfect for curious kids, families, and anyone who loves a little garden science.

All attendees must fill out the Saturdays on the Farm registration and complete the farm waiver: https://www.harlemgrown.org/saturdays-on-the-farm

classes.name: Writers Workshop at Bryant Park Reading Room
classes.desc:
Looking to craft a larger-than-life blockbuster? Or perhaps a down-to-earth indie is more your speed. Both require a great blueprint: the screenplay. Join us for this free workshop with Gotham Screenwriting instructor C.C. Webster to learn how to charge your stories with the visual verve of the silver screen. You'll learn some craft, do some writing, and get to hang out in the heart of the city with fellow aspiring screenwriters.

About the instructor
C.C. Webster wrote the feature films Blue Ball, PA, Show & Tell, and Little Buffalo, selected for the Writers Lab, a program sponsored by Meryl Streep and Nicole Kidman. Her feature script Missing won the American Scandinavian Society's Cultural Grant. She wrote and directed the short films "Tracks," "Easter," and "Civil War," which played at more than fifty film festivals worldwide. She directed the short film "Babies" and produced the short film "Junebug and Hurricane." She is the founder and artistic director of the Drive-In Film Festival. She has taught at Westchester Community College, Brandeis University, Columbia University, and Sarah Lawrence College. She holds a BA from Brandeis University, an MA from University College London, and an MFA in Directing and Screenwriting from Columbia University.

classes.name: Yoga at Bryant Park
classes.desc:
Everyone's favorite yoga program returns for summer 2026! From May 27 through September 16, join us for classes Tuesday mornings on the Upper Terrace, and Wednesday evenings on the lawn. Bryant Park Yoga presented by Halara is free to the public. Bring your mat and water and enjoy one hour of yoga with New York City's top instructors.

Please register for each class at our website online, or in person. Pre-registration is strongly encouraged. Those who pre-register opt in to receive updates on weather-related cancellations and other notifications. By pre-registering, you complete the waiver in advance and will receive weather-related updates.

Please present your QR code from your registration confirmation email to the Bryant Park check-in staff. Check-in locations can be found at all four corners of the lawn. Each check-in location is marked with pink barricades or a pink tent. We recommend you arrive at least 15 minutes early so you have time to check-in and roll out your mat before instruction begins.

Classes are cancelled only when it is deemed unsafe or uncomfortable for participants. Follow @bryantparknyc for day-of updates.

All Bryant Park Yoga classes are free and open to the public! All levels are welcome, thanks to the skill of our experienced teachers.

*Please note there will be NO CLASS on June 24 and July 1

classes.name: Yoga in the Park
classes.location: East River Picnic Area (in Randall's Island Park)
classes.desc:
Stretch, breathe, and find your inner peace at Randall's Island Park's free outdoor yoga series. Guided by a beloved, local yoga instructor, participants will flow through a calming and grounding sequence against the backdrop of the uptown skyline. All levels and abilities are welcome. Bring a friend, family member or two, or your yoga-loving pet!

No registration required–just show up ready to move! We'll have water and a limited number of mats available to borrow, so please bring your own if possible or show up early to claim one.

Our free yoga classes run weekly on Tuesdays from 6:30 – 7:30 pm from May 5 – September 1, and Saturdays from 10:00 – 11:00 am, July 11 – August 29. On Saturdays, stay after and join our dance class! Please check Instagram for the most updated schedule.

Location: East River Picnic Area

For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation, or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

classes.name: Yoga in the Park
classes.desc:
Exploratory yoga sessions, incorporating elements of Vinyasa and focusing on mindful movement. Pre-registration strongly suggested. Beginners welcome. BYOM: Bring Your Own Mat!
No experience needed, just bring a mat, water, and an open heart.

Van Cortlandt Park Memorial Grove

RSVP Now.

July 9 with Instructor Wendy of Yoga4theBx

Recharge and radiate like sunshine. We'll use breath to flow through a Vinyasa yoga practice.

Vinyasa yoga, also known as "flow yoga," is a dynamic style characterized by breath-synchronized movement, linking poses in a continuous, flowing sequence. It's known for its energetic nature!

July 16 with Instructor Ishkara Heqwabti

Calm your nervous system and feel consciously aware of the here and now.

This 60-minute class blends 30 minutes of Kemetic Yoga (an ancient Egyptian practice focused on breath and posture) and 30 minutes of immersive sound meditation using crystal singing bowls, voice and indigenous instruments that will leave you feeling grounded and renewed.

July 23 and 30 with Instructor Timothy Lewis

Reconnect with the natural world and your own inner landscape. Through mindful movement, breathwork, and stillness, we'll explore how the elements–earth, water, fire, air, and space live within the body.

August 6 with Instructor Melissa Rios

Find your flow! Looking for a refreshing way to wind down in the evening? Come join us where fresh air, sunset, and good vibes meet! Let's stretch, breathe, and move together under the open sky.
